items.title: From Data to Decisions: Automated Research Reporting Using Runner H AI Agents
items.author: Abu Taher Siddik
items.content:
I created an Automated Research and Insight Reporting workflow using Runner H that autonomously gathers the latest information on a specified topic from multiple trusted sources, summarizes key insights, categorizes findings, and compiles a well-structured report. The final report is automatically saved to Google Docs (or Notion) and shared with the team via Slack or email, complete with scheduled recurring updates.

This workflow solves the time-consuming and repetitive task of manual research and report generation, enabling professionals, researchers, and teams to stay informed effortlessly with up-to-date, curated insights delivered directly to their preferred platforms.

(If you have a video, embed it here using the markdown embed syntax)

Runner H’s multi-agent orchestration capabilities were key to this workflow:

  • Multi-step task decomposition: I defined subtasks for web scraping, API querying, summarization, categorization, document generation, and notifications.
  • Integration with external tools: Connected Runner H to Google Docs for report creation, Slack for team notifications, and calendar apps for scheduling recurring reports.
  • Runner H maintained context across all subtasks, ensuring coherent summaries and consistent report formatting.
  • Once triggered, the workflow runs end-to-end without manual intervention, freeing users from repetitive research tasks.

Instructions to replicate:

  1. Set your target research topic and preferred sources within the Runner H prompt.
  2. Connect your Google Docs, Slack, and calendar accounts via Runner H integrations.
  3. Trigger the workflow manually or schedule it to run automatically on a recurring basis.
  4. Receive the summarized research report directly in your team’s Slack channel and Google Docs folder.

This solution is ideal for:

  • who need to monitor fast-moving fields like AI, biotech, or finance.
  • Product managers and marketers seeking competitive intelligence and industry trends.
  • who want concise, actionable insights without spending hours on manual research.

By automating the entire research-to-report process, this workflow saves hours of tedious work weekly, reduces human error in data collection, and ensures timely, consistent delivery of high-quality insights. It transforms how knowledge workers stay informed, enabling smarter decisions and faster innovation.

1. Search the internet and specific trusted sources for the latest articles, papers, blog posts, and news related to [INSERT TOPIC, e.g., "Artificial Intelligence advancements"] published within the last [time frame, e.g., 7 days].

2. Prioritize reputable sources such as TechCrunch, Wired, arXiv, Medium, GitHub Trending, and relevant RSS feeds.

3. Extract key points, trends, and breakthroughs from each source, summarizing them in 2-3 sentences.

4. Categorize the findings into logical sections (e.g., Research Papers, Industry News, Tools & Projects).

5. Compile these summaries into a single cohesive report with:
   - A clear and engaging title
   - An introduction outlining the report's scope
   - Well-structured sections grouping related content
   - Concise, reader-friendly language suitable for [target audience, e.g., tech professionals]

6. Format the final report as a Markdown or Google Doc file, ready for distribution.

7. Save the report in [Google Drive/Notion/other] and notify the team via Slack/email with the report link.

8. Schedule a recurring calendar event for weekly/monthly report delivery.

Thank you to the Runner H team for this exciting challenge! Looking forward to seeing how the community innovates with AI agents.

items.title: The Best AI Tools: A Technical Perspective for Enthusiasts
items.author: Adeyemi Adetilewa
items.content:
Several years ago, AI tools seemed out of reach for most people. Today, almost anyone can access a smart assistant, experiment with text generation, or automate complex tasks without writing much code.

The pace of innovation is fast, but picking the best AI tools for your needs demands more than trending names or bold claims. Here’s a technical look at what matters and how to choose.

The Power of Specialization

All-in-one AI platforms have their place, but specialists now outperform generalists in many areas. For example, code assistants use language models fine-tuned for software development. Image generators have custom engines to understand artistic concepts. Whether you manage data, write copy, or need market research, you’ll find tools built with deep specialization.

To see what’s available, explore the AI Tools Directory. You’ll find categories covering image generation, productivity, content creation, and more.

Benchmarking: Beyond the Hype

Reliable AI tools back their claims with clear benchmarks. Look at public model reports, integration options, and openness of their algorithms. Modern tools publish latency, accuracy, and resource use. Transparency in these areas is a sign of maturity.

The best directories list real reviews and updated rankings based on independent tests. This helps filter hype from results.

AI shines brightest when automating routine or complex steps. Modern platforms link APIs, schedule daily tasks, and handle exceptions. If you’re comparing AI writing assistants, for example, look at which ones embed with your CMS or support multi-language output.

Workflows matter as much as raw output. If a tool promises high productivity, test its ease of integration and adaptability to your stack.

Content Creation: Quality Over Quantity

Text and image generators are everywhere, but not all meet professional standards. Advanced tools now offer tone adjustment, plagiarism detection, and tight collaboration features. These let teams edit, comment, and track revisions in real time.

The AI Tools for Content Creation section breaks down platforms for everything from copywriting to design. Verify whether tools use trusted models and follow content safety best practices.

Strong developer and user communities set leading AI tools apart. Open-source contributions, plugin ecosystems, and active forums extend a tool’s value far beyond its core features. Look for responsive support channels and a record of regular updates.

The “best” AI tool always depends on your goals, workflow, and technical comfort. Experienced users customize pipelines, automate tedious work, and fine-tune results through creative prompt engineering.

Explore, test, and compare before you commit. Consider bookmarking the Top AI Tools List for ongoing reference as new contenders arrive.

What’s the most important factor you look for in an AI tool? Speed, flexibility, transparency, or community? Drop your thoughts below and let’s discuss which features matter most in real work!

If you enjoyed this breakdown, share your experience or tag a friend who’s searching for the right AI fit.

items.title: Using Digital Avatars to Attract Talent
items.author: Engroso
items.content:
You might have seen many companies using AI Digital Avatars to hire. Some are big fans of this, some are not. Compared to the traditional hiring method, which requires lengthy interviews, high costs, time-consuming processes, and logistical hurdles, the Digital avatar method works and sounds better.

Many organizations, like Dropbox and Starbucks, have opted for video recruitment to showcase their work environments and core values.

Since AI avatars are a relatively new technology, adopting them requires a strategy. Let’s explore them.

What is a Digital Avatar?

A digital avatar is a virtual character generated through AI that interacts with users in real time. It is designed to resemble a realistic person or character. Their conversational abilities make them suitable for interactive roles, like guiding candidates in recruitment videos.

They can welcome applicants, provide information about the company, and answer common questions, giving candidates a feel for the company without requiring live interaction.

5 Tips for Using Digital Avatars in Recruitment Videos

Defining the purpose of your avatar-based recruitment videos is important. Without clear goals, the video may lack focus, affecting candidate engagement and data collection quality.

2. Choose the Right Avatar Personality

Selecting an avatar that aligns with your brand and the job role is key. A professional avatar can be the right option for technical roles, whereas a more relaxed one may be better suited for creative roles. Platforms like Simli offer a variety of avatars, and many tools allow for customization down to the voice.

3. Use AI to Generate Tailored Interview Questions

Automate interview questions with an AI tool like OpenAI's chat completions API. This approach lets you dynamically create questions based on a job description, making the interaction more personalized.

4. Integrate Lip-Synced Audio for Realistic Interactions

To create a natural feel in your videos, convert text-based questions to audio, then sync the audio with the avatar's lip movements.

5. Test and Iterate Your Videos

Before launching avatar-led interviews, conduct tests with a diverse group to gather feedback. Watch for awkward pauses, audio misalignments, or unclear prompts, and refine the script based on real interactions. Regularly update the avatar content to reflect current job demands, company culture, and candidate feedback.

Digital avatars reduce the stress and time recruiters spend using traditional methods in employee interviews and cut the cost of hiring more recruiters. Tools like Simli make it easy for companies to add AI avatars to their hiring process in just a few clicks.

items.title: AI Integration: Merging AI with Business Systems for Impact
items.author: Madison Carter
items.content:
To remain competitive in the rapidly evolving world of today, organizations must find clever solutions. Artificial intelligence integration, or the practice of combining AI with current business processes, is one such approach. Efficiency, decision-making, and general performance are all enhanced by this combination. For the smooth and efficient implementation of these cutting-edge technologies, many businesses are turning to expert AI development services.

AI integration is the process of integrating computer vision, machine learning, and natural language processing with business processes. It facilitates speedier data analysis, process automation, and improved client experiences. Commonplace systems, including supply chain management tools, accounting software, and CRM platforms, incorporate AI. It produces quicker results and more intelligent procedures when done correctly.

Benefits of AI Integration

Efficiency gains are among the main advantages of integrating AI. AI can perform repetitive activities like data entry and email sorting. Employees can concentrate on higher-value tasks and save time as a result.

Additionally, AI decreases human error, improving operational accuracy. Data analysis is an additional important advantage. AI tools can swiftly process vast volumes of data. They spot patterns and trends that people would take a lot longer to discover. This enables firms to use real-time data to make more informed decisions. It also makes planning and forecasting better.

Enhancing Customer Experience

Additionally, AI integration changes how companies communicate with their clientele. For instance, chatbots instantly respond to consumer inquiries. Working around the clock, they enhance reaction times. AI may also analyze consumer behavior to personalize user experiences. Higher satisfaction and improved engagement result from this.

AI in Different Business Areas

AI has potential benefits for various business divisions. AI in marketing can forecast which advertisements will be most successful. Finding odd trends in the financial industry aids in the detection of fraud. AI in HR is able to sift through job applications and find the best applicants. These are but a handful of instances of how the incorporation of AI is changing the way businesses operate.

Integration with AI has many benefits, but there are drawbacks as well. Companies must ensure the security and cleanliness of their data. They also require competent personnel to use AI tools. AI solutions might not always work with current systems. Expert assistance and careful preparation are needed for these problems.

Best Practices for AI Integration

Start with one process where AI could be useful, then grow it as necessary. Teams can make little adjustments and see tangible outcomes as a result. Partner with seasoned experts who can help you navigate the technical facets. To properly employ AI tools, make sure your team has received the necessary training. Additionally, whenever you use AI, keep ethics and data privacy in mind.

AI is more than simply a fad. It is becoming a fundamental aspect of how companies function. AI will develop more and be simpler to incorporate over time. Companies that make AI investments now will be more equipped for the future. They will have greater customer focus, agility, and responsiveness.

AI Integration is a Game Changer

Integration of AI has obvious effects. It boosts creativity, quickness, and accuracy. Businesses can use it to solve problems more intelligently. By integrating AI into their everyday operations, businesses may improve outcomes and open up new opportunities.

Integration of AI is changing how companies operate. Its advantages are numerous, ranging from task automation to better customer service. Businesses may take full advantage of this technology if they have the proper resources and strategy. Hire AI Developers who are knowledgeable about both the commercial and technical aspects of AI. They can assist you in coming up with strong ideas that have an impact that lasts.

items.title: Stop Wasting Time: Master AI Prompt Engineering Now
items.author: Praveen Sripati
items.content:
When I’m sitting down to integrate an AI into an application, I follow a pretty consistent mental checklist. It’s all about guiding the AI, step by step.

1. Know Your AI (and Its Quirks)

Before I even type my first prompt, I try to understand the AI model itself. Is it a language model, an image generator, or something else? What kind of data was it trained on? Knowing its strengths (and more importantly, its weaknesses) helps me set realistic expectations. I also dig into the API documentation to see what parameters I can tweak — things like (how creative or "safe" the response is) or (how long the response can be). These are my knobs and dials for fine-tuning.

Treat your AI like a junior developer: give it clear instructions, context, and examples, and it’ll surprise you with what it can achieve.

2. Be Crystal Clear and Super Specific

This is the golden rule I live by. Vague prompts are a recipe for unpredictable results. I always ask myself: “Am I telling the AI exactly what I want?”

  • Instead of “Write about climate change,” I’d write, “Summarize the key environmental impacts of climate change in 3 concise bullet points for a general audience.” See the difference? I’m telling it what to do, what topic, how many points, and for whom.
  • If I need JSON, I ask for JSON. If I need Markdown, I specify Markdown. This is crucial for me because I’m usually processing the AI’s output programmatically. I’ll often say something like, “Provide a JSON object with name, age, and city fields."
  • Need a short response? I’ll add “under 100 words.” No jargon? I’ll explicitly state, “Do not use technical jargon.”

The best prompts aren’t just questions; they’re precise contracts with the AI.

3. Provide Context and Examples

AI models aren’t mind-readers. The more context I give them, the better they perform.

  • If I’m building a customer support bot, I’ll start the prompt with something like, “You are a customer support agent for a software company. The user is experiencing a login error with code ‘AUTH-001’.” This sets the stage.
  • This is a powerful technique. I include a few input-output examples directly in my prompt. For instance, if I want a specific translation style, I’ll show it: 
Translate English to French: 
English: Hello 
French: Bonjour 

English: Thank you 
French: Merci 

English: Good morning 
French: ---

This teaches the AI the pattern I expect.

Think of few-shot prompting as giving the AI a quick, personalized training session — right inside your prompt.

4. Leverage Advanced Techniques

For more complex tasks, I pull out some of my more advanced tricks:

  • If I need the AI to reason or solve a multi-step problem, I’ll ask it to “think step by step” or “show your reasoning.” This makes its process transparent and often leads to more accurate answers. For example, “Solve this math problem. Show your step-by-step reasoning: (10 + 5) * 2.”
  • Sometimes, I need the AI to adopt a specific persona. If I need a technical explanation, I’ll tell it, “Act as a senior software engineer.” This helps the AI use appropriate language and knowledge.
  • For longer prompts with different sections (instructions, context, input), I use clear separators like ###, ---, or even XML-like tags (, ). This helps the AI understand the structure of my prompt.

Chain-of-Thought isn’t just about getting the right answer; it’s about making the AI’s logic traceable and debuggable.

5. My Iterative Workflow (Test, Refine, Repeat!)

This is where the rubber meets the road. Prompt engineering is almost never a one-shot deal.

  1. I start with a simple prompt.
  2. I send it to the AI API and look at the response.
  3. Did it meet my expectations? Is the format right? Is the content accurate?
  4. Based on the evaluation, I tweak the prompt. Maybe I need to add more context, clarify an instruction, or change a parameter like temperature.
  5. I keep going back to step 2 until I’m satisfied. It’s an ongoing process of trying, learning, and improving.

Prompt engineering is less about finding the magic words and more about embracing the iterative loop.”

6. Mind the Ethics and Bias

As a developer, I also keep ethical considerations in mind. AI models can inherit biases from their training data, so I try to craft prompts that encourage fair, inclusive, and unbiased responses. I also think about safety guardrails to prevent the AI from generating inappropriate or harmful content.

While understanding these principles is crucial, I know that for developers, seeing code in action makes all the difference. That’s why I’ve put together a dedicated follow-up post. In ‘Prompt Engineering in Action: Examples using Google Gemini & Node.js’, I dive deep into practical Node.js examples using the Google Gemini API, showing you exactly how to implement these techniques in your applications. Head over there to start building!

items.title: How to Use a Modded Version of TikTok Properly: A Complete Guide
items.author: Mike Anderson
items.content:
How to Use a Modded Version of TikTok Properly TikTok is one of the most popular social media platforms, but its official app comes with limitations—ads, regional restrictions, and a lack of advanced features. That’s where modded versions of TikTok come in. These modified APKs offer enhanced functionalities, but using them improperly can lead to account bans or security risks.

In this guide, we’ll walk you through everything you need to know about safely using a Tiktok MOD APK for free, from installation to best practices.

  1. What Is a Modded TikTok APK? A modded TikTok APK is an altered version of the original app, created by third-party developers to unlock premium features without paying. These modifications can include:

No ads – Skip annoying commercials.

Unlocked region-restricted content – Access videos from anywhere.

Download videos without watermarks – Save content directly.

Increased exposure – Fake likes, views, or followers (though risky).

But before diving in, it’s crucial to understand both the perks and the dangers.

  1. Benefits of Using a Modded TikTok Version Enhanced User Experience Modded TikTok versions eliminate many frustrations of the official app. Imagine scrolling without constant ad interruptions or downloading your favorite clips in HD—sounds great, right?

Access to Premium Features for Free Some mods unlock paid effects, filters, and editing tools, giving you a premium experience at zero cost.

Bypassing Geo-Restrictions If certain trends or sounds are blocked in your country, a modded APK might help you access them.

  1. Risks and Precautions When Using Modded TikTok While modded apps offer tempting features, they come with serious risks:

Account Bans – TikTok’s algorithm detects suspicious activity, and mods can trigger permanent bans.

Malware & Data Theft – Unofficial APKs may contain spyware or viruses.

No Official Support – If something goes wrong, you’re on your own.

How to Stay Safe ✔ Download from trusted sources (like reputable modding forums).

✔ Use a secondary account (avoid linking your main profile).

✔ Install antivirus software (scan APKs before installing).

  1. How to Download and Install Modded TikTok Step 1: Enable Unknown Sources Since modded APKs aren’t on the Play Store, you’ll need to:

Go to Settings > Security.

Enable "Install Unknown Apps" for your browser/file manager.

Step 2: Download a Reliable Mod APK Look for trusted sites like APKovo(best choice) or APKMirror (avoid shady download links).

Step 3: Install and Configure Open the downloaded APK and tap Install.

Log in with a burner account (not your primary one).

  1. Best Practices for Safe Usage To minimize risks, follow these tips:

Avoid excessive automation (fake likes/views can get you flagged).

Don’t use mods for monetized accounts (TikTok may ban you).

Update cautiously – New mod versions may fix bugs or introduce new ones.

  1. Conclusion Using a MOD APK of Tiktok can enhance your experience, but it’s not without risks. By following this guide—downloading safely, using a secondary account, and avoiding suspicious behavior—you can enjoy unlocked features while minimizing dangers.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

  1. Is using a modded version of TikTok legal? Modding itself isn’t illegal, but it violates TikTok’s terms of service, risking account bans.

  2. Can I get banned for using a TikTok mod? Yes, if TikTok detects modified app behavior, your account could be suspended.

  3. Which is the safest modded TikTok APK? ProMod, TikTok Lite Mod, and TikMod are among the more reliable options.

  4. Do modded versions work on iOS? No, iOS requires jailbreaking, which is riskier than Android modding.

  5. Can I download TikTok videos without a watermark with a mod? Yes, most mods include this feature.

  6. Will a VPN help avoid bans with a modded TikTok? A VPN can mask your location but won’t prevent bans from mod detection.

  7. Are modded TikTok apps free? Yes, but some may contain hidden malware—always verify sources.

By staying informed and cautious, you can make the most of modded TikTok while keeping your account secure. Happy scrolling!

items.title: The AI & Blockchain Revolution: Reshaping the API Economy
items.author: Coder
items.content:
The API economy is in a state of rapid evolution, moving beyond simple data exchange to become the backbone of intelligent, decentralized, and highly automated software ecosystems. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Blockchain technology are at the forefront of this transformation, fundamentally reshaping how APIs are designed, secured, and utilized in modern software. This article explores the profound impact of AI and Blockchain on APIs, highlighting emerging trends, practical applications, and the opportunities they present for developers and businesses.

The Rise of Intelligent APIs: AI as an API Consumer and Enabler

AI's integration with APIs is a two-way street: APIs consume AI services, and AI is increasingly used to enhance and manage APIs themselves. The explosion of AI adoption in 2024 has directly fueled a massive surge in API usage, as applications leverage APIs to access sophisticated AI and Machine Learning (ML) capabilities without building complex models in-house. According to Postman's 2024 State of the API Report, AI-related traffic across their platform increased by nearly 73% in 2024, emphasizing the growing reliance on APIs for AI functionalities.

How AI is Reshaping APIs:

  • These APIs can understand and process natural language, images, and unstructured data, enabling applications to perform human-like comprehension tasks. Think of APIs that offer sentiment analysis, image recognition, or intelligent document processing.
  • AI-Powered API Optimization: AI algorithms can analyze API usage patterns, performance metrics, and even security logs to automatically adjust API behavior, optimize routing, and identify potential issues in real-time.
  • Enhanced User Experiences: AI-driven APIs enable more personalized and responsive user experiences by tailoring responses based on user context and behavior. This is crucial for dynamic chatbots, recommendation engines, and adaptive interfaces.
  • APIs act as the connective tissue for hyperautomation, integrating various AI, ML, and Robotic Process Automation (RPA) tools to create end-to-end automated workflows. This can significantly reduce operational costs and improve process accuracy. The API Conference highlights hyperautomation as a key trend for 2025, noting that APIs provide the standardized interfaces for connecting diverse automation tools.

Example: AI-Powered Text Summarization API

Imagine an API that takes a long text as input and returns a concise summary using an AI model.

Decentralized Trust: Blockchain's Influence on API Interactions

Blockchain technology is introducing new paradigms of security, transparency, and trust to the API economy. By leveraging distributed ledgers and cryptographic principles, Blockchain is enabling novel ways to manage API access, transactions, and data integrity. The API Conference's "API Economy: Trends and Transformations for 2025" report identifies the integration of blockchain technology with APIs as a key trend, leading to secure, transparent, and decentralized digital services.

How Blockchain is Impacting APIs:

  • Access to APIs can be controlled and monetized using blockchain-based tokens, allowing for granular access management and new revenue streams.
  • Blockchain can provide immutable records of API calls and data exchanges, enhancing auditability and trust in multi-party interactions, especially in regulated industries like finance and healthcare.
  • Blockchain-based decentralized identity systems can offer secure, privacy-preserving authentication and authorization for API consumers, reducing reliance on centralized identity providers.
  • Smart Contracts for API Agreements: Smart contracts can automate and enforce the terms of API usage agreements, including payment, service level agreements (SLAs), and data sharing rules, reducing the need for intermediaries.

Conceptual Example: Blockchain-Enabled API Access

A smart contract on a blockchain could manage subscriptions and grant access to a premium API.

// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.0;

contract PremiumAPIAccess {
    address public apiProvider;
    mapping(address => bool) public hasAccess;
    mapping(address => uint256) public accessExpiry;

    event AccessGranted(address indexed user, uint256 expiryTime);
    event AccessRevoked(address indexed user);

    constructor() {
        apiProvider = msg.sender;
    }

    modifier onlyApiProvider() {
        require(msg.sender == apiProvider, "Only API provider can call this function");
        _;
    }

    function grantAccess(address _user, uint256 _durationInDays) public onlyApiProvider {
        accessExpiry[_user] = block.timestamp + (_durationInDays * 1 days);
        hasAccess[_user] = true;
        emit AccessGranted(_user, accessExpiry[_user]);
    }

    function revokeAccess(address _user) public onlyApiProvider {
        hasAccess[_user] = false;
        accessExpiry[_user] = 0;
        emit AccessRevoked(_user);
    }

    function checkAccess(address _user) public view returns (bool) {
        return hasAccess[_user] && (accessExpiry[_user] > block.timestamp);
    }
}

Note: The above is a Solidity smart contract example, not Python. It illustrates the concept of blockchain-enabled API access management.

Synergies and the Future Landscape

The true power lies in the synergy between AI and Blockchain within the API ecosystem. AI can optimize the performance and security of blockchain-enabled APIs, while blockchain can provide the trust and transparency needed for AI-driven automated processes. This convergence is paving the way for:

  • Highly Secure and Compliant APIs: AI can detect anomalous patterns in API traffic to prevent attacks, while blockchain can ensure the integrity of API data and transactions, addressing critical security and compliance concerns. "Security-first API design" is a fundamental shift, incorporating zero-trust principles and advanced encryption, as highlighted by the API Conference.
  • Tokenization and smart contracts enable innovative API monetization strategies, allowing for micro-payments, usage-based billing, and decentralized marketplaces.
  • Advanced Hyperautomation: The combination of AI for intelligent decision-making and blockchain for verifiable execution can lead to truly autonomous and trustworthy automated workflows.

Challenges and Considerations

Despite the immense potential, integrating AI and Blockchain with APIs presents challenges:

  • The lack of universal API standards can hinder seamless integration and interoperability, especially as new AI and Blockchain-specific API patterns emerge. Nordic APIs' "Shift to AI Exploded API Usage in 2024" report notes that the lack of standardization can cause security issues and poor performance.
  • Blockchain transactions can introduce latency, which needs to be carefully managed for real-time API interactions.
  • Designing, developing, and managing AI and Blockchain-enabled APIs requires specialized skills and a deeper understanding of these complex technologies.
  • While both technologies enhance security, their integration also introduces new attack vectors that need to be addressed with robust security-first design principles. The Nordic APIs report indicates that the average API has a low security score, with many lacking authentication or SSL/TLS encryption.

The convergence of AI and Blockchain is not merely an incremental improvement for APIs; it's a fundamental shift towards a more intelligent, secure, and decentralized digital future. Organizations that embrace these trends and proactively invest in developing and managing AI and Blockchain-enabled APIs will be well-positioned to unlock new levels of innovation, efficiency, and trust in the evolving software landscape. For a deeper understanding of the foundational role of APIs in modern software, explore this comprehensive guide: The Role of APIs in Modern Software.

items.content:

TYPO3, a powerful enterprise-level CMS, is stepping boldly into this future with its groundbreaking TYPO3 AI capabilities, also known as TYPO3 KI in the German-speaking community.

In this blog, we explore how TYPO3 AI, especially through the TYPO3 AI Extension, is transforming the CMS landscape for editors, developers, businesses, and end users.

*Why AI Matters in Modern CMS Platforms * Traditional CMSs have been limited to manual tasks: content writing, layout adjustments, SEO tweaks, and accessibility management. This manual process is time-consuming and error-prone.

That’s where Artificial Intelligence (AI) enters the picture. AI enables automation, improves accuracy, enhances personalization, and provides intelligent content insights.

*Introducing TYPO3 AI: Smart CMS for Smart Teams * TYPO3 AI, often referred to as TYPO3 KI in German, brings next-level intelligence to content management. items.title: The Automated Opportunity Scout: How Runner H Became My Personal Business Analyst
items.author: miku iwai
items.content:
I built the Automated Opportunity Scout, an autonomous AI agent that acts as my personal business development analyst.

As a freelancer, my biggest non-billable time sink was prospecting. I used to spend over 10 hours a week manually searching for new clients, a repetitive process that yielded inconsistent results. The problem wasn't just finding data; it was connecting the dots to find real, actionable opportunities.

My Runner H workflow solves this problem entirely. Each morning, it autonomously scans the web for key business signals (like company funding announcements), identifies promising target companies, conducts deep-dive research to find the right decision-maker, analyzes their needs, and compiles a complete strategic briefing—including pre-drafted, personalized outreach emails. It turns hours of manual drudgery into a single, powerful morning briefing delivered straight to my inbox.

Seeing is believing. The screenshots below demonstrate the seamless magic of the workflow from a single prompt to a final, actionable brief.

After inputting the master prompt.

  • The Final Generated Briefing: This is the Google Doc created by Runner H—a polished, ready-to-use strategic brief.

I leveraged Runner H's ability to act as a true agent by chaining together multiple tools and complex instructions into a single, powerful workflow. The agent uses Google Search for research, Google Docs for synthesis, and Gmail for notification.

The entire automation is powered by one .

Act as my dedicated 'Opportunity Scout' for my freelance web design business specializing in e-commerce for sustainable brands.

Your mission is to perform the following tasks and compile the results into a new Google Doc titled '[Today's Date] - Opportunity Briefing'.

1.  SCAN FOR LEADS: Search Google News, tech blogs, and press release sites for announcements in the last 48 hours using keywords like "new sustainable CPG brand," "eco-friendly startup funding," and "new Shopify Plus store."

2.  IDENTIFY KEY TARGETS: From the search results, identify 3-5 promising companies that have either just received funding, announced a new product line, or have a poorly designed website.

3.  CONDUCT DEEP-DIVE RESEARCH: For each identified company, perform the following:
    a. Find their company website and LinkedIn page.
    b. Identify a key decision-maker, such as 'Head of Marketing', 'Founder', or 'Head of E-commerce'. Note their full name and title.
    c. Briefly analyze their current website, looking for one major area of improvement (e.g., slow loading speed, poor mobile experience, confusing navigation).

4.  DRAFT OUTREACH: For each target, draft a short, personalized outreach email. The email should:
    a. Congratulate them on their recent news (the funding, launch, etc.).
    b. Briefly introduce my services as a web designer for sustainable brands.
    c. Mention the specific area of improvement you identified in a helpful, non-critical way.
    d. End with a clear call to action for a brief chat.
    e. **Do NOT send the email. Place each draft in the briefing document.**

5.  COMPILE THE BRIEFING: Create the final Google Doc. Structure it clearly with the following sections for each opportunity:
    *   Company Name:
    *   Website & LinkedIn URL:
    *   Key Contact: [Name, Title]
    *   The Opportunity: (e.g., "Recently raised $2M seed round.")
    *   Website Analysis Note: (e.g., "Product pages take over 5 seconds to load on mobile.")
    *   DRAFT Outreach Email: [Paste the drafted email here]

6.  FINAL NOTIFICATION: Once the Google Doc is created and populated, send an email to my address `your-email@gmail.com` with the subject "Your Daily Opportunity Briefing is Ready!" and the body containing a link to the new Google Doc.

How to Replicate This Workflow:

  1. Ensure your Runner H account has access to your Google account (for Docs and Gmail).
  2. In Runner H, start a new task.
  3. Copy the "Master Prompt" above and paste it into Runner H.
  4. Change the details like your role (), niche (), keywords, and email address to fit your own needs.
  5. Execute the agent and wait for the notification email to arrive in your inbox.

This solution demonstrates the transformative power of AI agents beyond simple task automation.

  • Who would benefit from this? This is a game-changer for freelancers, consultants, small agency owners, and business development teams. Anyone whose success depends on finding and acting on new opportunities can adapt this workflow.

  • How does it improve existing processes? It replaces over 10 hours per week of manual, low-value prospecting with a 5-minute task of reviewing a high-value strategic brief. More importantly, it shifts the entire process from being (scrolling through job boards) to (identifying opportunities before they even become public job listings). This provides a massive strategic advantage, allowing the user to be the first to reach out with a highly relevant, personalized message.

I shared this project to show the developer and creator community what's possible when you delegate your most time-consuming tasks to an AI agent like Runner H.

items.title: How Agentic AI Companies Create Goal-Oriented Intelligent Agents
items.author: David J
items.content:
The future of enterprise automation is increasingly being shaped by Agentic AI—a new paradigm in artificial intelligence where systems act with autonomy, memory, planning, and purpose. Unlike traditional AI models, which operate as tools for inference or classification, agentic AI systems operate as intelligent entities that pursue goals over time. This shift is transforming industries from manufacturing to customer service, and companies that specialize in are at the forefront of this movement.

Whether it’s a web ai agent that provides real-time decision support, a manufacturing ai agent that coordinates robotic operations on a factory floor, or a sales ai agent that autonomously nurtures leads, these intelligent systems share one common trait—they are built to achieve specific goals without constant human oversight.

This article explores how agentic AI companies build these sophisticated agents, highlighting the architectures, methodologies, and strategies that lead to functional and safe goal-oriented AI.

What Is a Goal-Oriented Intelligent Agent?

A goal-oriented intelligent agent is a system that:

  • provided in natural or symbolic form
  • Perceives its environment through structured or unstructured data
  • over possible actions
  • based on success, failure, and changing conditions

Such agents differ from static AI models because they operate in dynamic contexts, make autonomous decisions, and actively pursue end goals, not just respond to queries.

In enterprise settings, goal-oriented agents are being used to:

  • Automate document processing workflows
  • Optimize supply chain logistics
  • Personalize customer journeys
  • Schedule maintenance for industrial equipment
  • Assist in research, writing, and content curation

Core Components of Goal-Oriented Agentic AI Systems

Agentic AI companies use modular architectures that enable autonomy while ensuring control. A typical agent includes the following:

The goal manager interprets user-defined goals or system objectives. For example, in a , the goal might be “increase qualified leads in EMEA by 20%.” The agent translates this into subgoals and tasks.

Agents require situational awareness. This component ingests data—user interactions, sensor inputs, CRM records, or web content—and builds a context model. A , for instance, might perceive user behavior, site navigation patterns, and past conversation history.

Using decision trees, symbolic reasoning, reinforcement learning, or other AI methods, the agent determines the best course of action to fulfill its goal. often use temporal and constraint-based planning for factory operations.

Unlike stateless bots, agentic AI requires short- and long-term memory. This allows agents to retain context across sessions, learn from past actions, and adjust future strategies.

Agents can execute actions via APIs, software interfaces, or physical actuators. A , for example, might interact with GitHub, Jenkins, and cloud infrastructure to deploy a software service.

6.

To improve over time, agents evaluate their outcomes. If a path fails, they try alternatives, creating a feedback loop essential for adaptation and growth.

How AI Agent Development Companies Design Goal-Oriented Agents

Agentic AI companies follow structured pipelines when designing and deploying intelligent agents. Here are key stages in the process:

It starts with understanding the business need in terms of goals. Is the agent supporting users, improving operations, or automating decision-making? This framing defines what success looks like.

  • A must reduce machine downtime.
  • A must increase qualified lead conversion.
  • A web ai agent must reduce bounce rates through proactive assistance.

Developers then model the environment the agent operates in:

  • What are its inputs and outputs?
  • What actions can it take?
  • How are states transitioned?

This is especially important for agents operating in complex systems like ERP software or e-commerce sites.

Goals are broken down into manageable tasks. For example, “Increase conversion rate” becomes:

  • Personalize communication
  • Prioritize based on behavior

Each sub-task is assigned to sub-agents or functional modules.

4. Integration with Tools and APIs

Goal-oriented agents rely on tools to act. Agentic AI companies integrate the system with:

  • Web services (for web ai agents)
  • ERP/CRM (for sales ai agents)
  • PLCs/SCADA systems (for manufacturing ai agents)
  • DevOps pipelines (for build ai agent tools)

These integrations expand the agent’s action space.

5. Memory and Context Modeling

Agents are given memory—using vector databases, semantic graphs, or local storage—to remember:

  • Dialogue or interaction history

This makes agents consistent, adaptive, and intelligent over time.

Safety and alignment are critical. Guardrails are implemented via:

  • Human-in-the-loop confirmations

This ensures the agent never drifts from acceptable boundaries.

Before going live, agent behavior is tested in sandbox environments. This exposes edge cases and unintended consequences, ensuring robustness.

Real-World Examples of Goal-Oriented Agentic AI

1. Customer Support – Web AI Agent

A telecom company implemented a web ai agent that doesn't just answer FAQs, but actively reduces churn by offering personalized upgrade paths, flagging frustration signals, and escalating high-risk users to human reps. Its goal is not to end the chat but to improve retention metrics.

2. Industrial Automation – Manufacturing AI Agent

An electronics manufacturer used a manufacturing ai agent to schedule predictive maintenance. The agent learns failure patterns, allocates technicians based on skill and proximity, and adjusts production schedules to avoid bottlenecks. It operates with a single goal: maximize uptime.

3. Sales Optimization – Sales AI Agent

A global SaaS firm deployed a sales ai agent that qualifies leads, writes tailored outreach emails, and books demos. It dynamically adjusts targeting strategies based on campaign performance, helping sales teams focus only on high-intent accounts.

Benefits of Goal-Oriented Agents for Enterprises

  • : Agents independently manage tasks, reducing manual workload.
  • : Goal-based design drives agents to optimize for success.
  • : Once trained, agents can be deployed across multiple environments.
  • : With memory and feedback loops, agents evolve with usage.
  • : Early adopters of agentic AI see faster innovation cycles.

Challenges in Building Goal-Oriented AI Agents

Multi-step planning under uncertainty remains a major technical hurdle, especially in open environments like the web.

2. Balancing Autonomy and Control

Too much freedom can lead to unexpected behavior; too little autonomy limits utility.

Agents that make decisions using private or regulated data must comply with privacy laws and ethical guidelines.

Users must trust that the agent understands context, respects preferences, and doesn’t act in opaque ways.

With advancements in LLMs, memory frameworks, multi-agent systems, and , agentic AI will only become more powerful. Agent marketplaces, reusable agent templates, and platform-based development will make building agents faster and more accessible.

In 2025 and beyond, every enterprise will likely have dozens of agents handling internal workflows, customer engagement, analytics, and even strategy execution. Agentic AI companies that build with transparency, safety, and modularity will lead this transition.

Goal-oriented intelligent agents represent a leap forward in enterprise AI. By combining perception, planning, action, and memory, these systems are revolutionizing how businesses operate, make decisions, and scale services.

Whether you’re deploying a for customer interactions, a for pipeline optimization, a for plant operations, or choosing to solutions from scratch—ethics, design structure, and aligned goals are key.

Companies focused on ai agent development are not just building tools—they are crafting digital colleagues. As enterprise needs evolve, so too will the autonomy and intelligence of these agents—ushering in a new era of distributed, goal-driven enterprise AI.

items.title: From Prompt to Play: Battleship Game Tutorial using Amazon Q, React and TypeScript
items.author: Axel Dlv
items.content:
I recently came across this AWS Community post, which inspired me to build a classic using , , and the . Battleship is a timeless strategy game I loved as a kid, and it felt like the perfect choice for showcasing how Amazon Q can assist with building interactive browser-based games using modern web technologies.

I chose Battleship because it’s a nostalgic favorite from my childhood. I remember playing it on those plastic game boards with miniature ships. The rules are simple but strategic: you hide your ships, take turns firing shots, and try to sink the opponent’s fleet.

That made it perfect for a small web game, it’s self-contained, logical, and full of fun UI possibilities. I also liked the challenge of designing an opponent AI that could be both fair and challenging.

How to Use Amazon Q Like I Did

You’ll first need to install the Amazon Q Developer CLI. It’s a powerful tool that lets you chat with Amazon Q directly from your local environment or IDE.

Once installed, you can use right from your terminal to generate, debug, and improve code just like I did.

Effective Prompting Techniques I Learned

I started with a "clear" and "detailed" prompt for Amazon Q (in terminal, ) :

"Create a browser-based Battleship game for one player against AI. The game should follow standard Battleship rules (a beautiful 10x10 grid, ships logo with the size of 5, 4, 3, 3, and 2), with players taking turns.

The game should work in the browser without page reloads.

The player should have their own board to place ships and track hits/misses on the opponent’s board.

Add simple UI to place ships manually, and a start button to begin the game.

Handle game state for human player (ships placed, hits/misses, current turn).

Show a simple message or modal when a player wins.

Use TypeScript and a React front-end framework."

I found that prompt quality directly impacts code quality.

  • Be clear about the frontend tech (React + TypeScript)
  • Describe UI expectations and game flow in plain language

Later, I refined the prompt to add new logic:

"Can you add which ship is destroyed during the game ?"

This small change resulted in a useful UX improvement.

Now, the game tells you exactly which ship you’ve sunk, not just that you hit something.

How AI Handled Classic Programming Challenges

Amazon Q handled several programming challenges surprisingly well:

  • Ship placement validation: Making sure no ships overlap and all stay within grid bounds
  • Turn-based logic: Enforcing correct sequence of moves
  • AI logic: The bot fires intelligently and doesn’t reuse previous moves
  • Game state tracking: Managing separate boards, hit/miss states, and victory conditions

When I introduced a bug by modifying the ship destruction logic, I hit an error:

Module parse failed: Duplicate export 'default' (354:7)

I asked Q to fix it, and it correctly identified and removed the redundant export. That ability to both generate and debug code made development feel smoother than traditional trial-and-error debugging.

AI-Generated Code Examples (on GitHub)

Rather than flood this post with code, You can find the code below.

Here’s what you can expect:

  • Ship placement screen with click-to-place
  • Real-time feedback on hits and misses and "You sunk my Battleship!" messages.
  • End-of-game modal with reset option

This was a great hands-on project that combined frontend UI, game logic, and AI collaboration. Using Amazon Q Chat for code generation, debugging, and logic enhancement was a genuinely productive experience.

Whether you’re building games, dashboards, or apps. AI + React is a serious combo, and tools like Amazon Q make it even better.

items.title: Inside the Research: A Detailed Technical Breakdown of SQD in Quantum Chemistry
items.author: Quambase
items.content:
This research introduces Sample-Based Quantum Diagonalization (SQD) integrated with implicit solvent models to enable accurate and scalable simulations of molecular systems as they exist in real-world environments. Rather than idealized vacuum conditions, this framework accounts for the influence of solvents — an essential step for making quantum chemical simulations applicable to real-life chemistry, biology, and materials science.

Realistic Simulation of Molecular Systems Traditional quantum simulations in computational chemistry are typically performed under the assumption that the molecule exists in a vacuum. However, in real-world applications, molecules often exist in solvent environments — such as water, alcohols, or other liquid media — which significantly influence:

    Accounting for these solvent effects is computationally expensive and, until now, not well-suited for quantum computation. Existing quantum simulation frameworks are limited in scope and fail to offer practical scalability when applied to solvated systems.

    Methodological Innovation: Sample-Based Quantum Diagonalization (SQD) To address these limitations, the researchers propose a novel framework known as Sample-Based Quantum Diagonalization (SQD). SQD integrates quantum computing techniques with implicit solvation models to approximate how solvents affect the properties of molecules, without explicitly simulating every solvent molecule.

    1. Implicit Solvation via IEF-PCM The Integral Equation Formalism Polarizable Continuum Model (IEF-PCM) is an established classical technique that treats the solvent as a continuous polarizable dielectric medium rather than simulating individual solvent molecules. The solute (molecule of interest) is placed within a cavity embedded in this polarizable continuum.

    This model calculates the interaction between the solute’s electron density and the surrounding solvent, enabling accurate simulation of solvent-induced polarization effects without incurring excessive computational cost.

    Integrating IEF-PCM into the quantum simulation workflow allows the quantum system to account for environmental effects realistically and efficiently.

    2. Active Space Selection for Efficient Simulation Quantum simulations of entire molecules, especially those with many electrons, are infeasible with current hardware. Therefore, active space methods are used to reduce the simulation domain to only the most chemically relevant orbitals and electrons.

    In this study, active space configurations were selected for various test molecules:

    **- Methanol (CH₃OH): 14 electrons, 12 orbitals

    • Methylamine (CH₃NH₂): 14 electrons, 13 orbitals
    • Ethanol (C₂H₅OH): 20 electrons, 18 orbitals
    • Water (H₂O): 8 electrons, 23 orbitals**

    This reduction allows for detailed quantum treatment of the molecule’s electronic structure without overwhelming quantum resources.

    This strategy balances chemical accuracy and quantum feasibility, making quantum simulations practical even for moderately complex molecules.

    1. LUCIJ Quantum Circuits** The Low-depth Unitary Coupled Cluster with Iterative Jacobi Rotations (LUCIJ) framework is employed to efficiently diagonalize the system Hamiltonian using quantum gates.

    LUCIJ circuits are optimized for:

    **- Low circuit depth, reducing decoherence issues

    • Efficient representation of the molecular wavefunction
    • Adaptability to various molecular configurations and active space selections**

    These circuits employ Jacobi rotations, a mathematical approach for matrix diagonalization, in a format compatible with quantum gate-based computation.

    LUCIJ circuits allow accurate quantum simulations while minimizing hardware requirements and error rates.

    4. Sample-Based Configuration Averaging A distinguishing feature of SQD is the use of sample-based statistical averaging over molecular configurations. Instead of running a single deterministic simulation, the method generates between 1⁰³ and 1⁰⁶ configurations of the solute-solvent system using the implicit solvation model and samples from this configuration space.

    The quantum simulation is then applied across these samples, and results are statistically aggregated.

    This approach improves robustness, provides a natural mechanism for error mitigation, and ensures that results reflect ensemble-averaged behavior typical of solvated molecules.

    5. Hybrid Quantum-Classical Workflow The simulation framework integrates classical and quantum computing components to optimize resource usage:

    Classical components perform:

    **- Geometry optimization

    • Implicit solvent modeling (IEF-PCM)
    • Quantum components execute:
    • Hamiltonian construction and diagonalization
    • Energy and observable calculations using LUCIJ circuits**

    This division of labor capitalizes on the strengths of both paradigms and makes the overall process executable on currently available quantum hardware.

    Technical Metrics and ResultsThe researchers achieved the following benchmarks:

    **- Energy accuracy within 0.1–0.5 kcal/mol of high-level classical methods (e.g., CCSD)

    • Computational efficiency improved by up to 60% over previous quantum simulation approaches
    • CNOT gate counts optimized to reduce quantum error, while maintaining accuracy
    • Convergence rates showed systematic improvement with increased sample sizes, validating the scalability of the sampling approach**

    Applications and Real-World Impact Immediate Applications:

    **- Pharmaceutical R&D: Accurately simulating how drug molecules behave in biological fluids (e.g., blood, cytoplasm)

    • Catalyst Design: Engineering industrial catalysts with solvent-sensitive performance
    • Environmental Chemistry: Modeling pollutant behavior in aquatic systems
    • Materials Science: Developing polymers, membranes, and other materials with solvent-responsive properties**

    Simulating drug interactions within individual biochemical environments

    Designing low-toxicity, sustainable chemical processes

    Energy Storage: *Improving battery electrolytes and fuel cells by simulating solvent-ion interactions * Scientific and Technological Contributions First successful integration of implicit solvent modeling with quantum diagonalization techniques

    Demonstrated scalable framework adaptable to a wide range of molecular systems

    Established a clear quantum advantage in simulating chemically realistic environments

    Open-source release ensures reproducibility and community collaboration

    **- Application of geometry optimization in solvent-aware quantum frameworks

    • Deployment of error mitigation strategies for noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices
    • Implementation of parallel quantum circuit execution for enhanced throughput
    • Benchmarking against high-level classical methods for validation**

    Toward Practical Quantum Chemistry This research provides a foundational step toward making quantum chemistry practical, scalable, and solvent-aware. By combining implicit solvation models, optimized quantum circuits, and statistical sampling, the authors offer a method that bridges theoretical simulations with real-world chemical behavior.

    It lays the groundwork for a future where quantum-enhanced drug discovery, green manufacturing, and next-generation materials are not just theoretically possible, but scientifically achievable.
