items.title: How to Do Netgear Orbi Setup with Existing Router with Ease?
items.author: Orbi Login
items.content:
A lot of people desire to make their home Wi-Fi better without getting rid of or changing their router. The Netgear Orbi system is meant to improve and expand wifi coverage. It may operate with your existing router if you set it up correctly. The best way to set up the Orbi is in Access Point Mode, not the Router Mode, as operating both devices in Router Mode might cause problems with the network.

This lets the Orbi work just as a wireless extender. Your current router will handle routing, while the Orbi will handle Wi-Fi distribution. This makes sure that the performance is steady, that there are no double NAT issues, and that the signal intensity stays the same in all rooms or floors. This article will show you how to set up your Netgear Orbi with an existing router. It will cover everything from connecting the devices physically to logging into the Orbi router and making the required changes to make sure your wireless network is stable and works well.

Introduction: Why Pair Orbi with an Existing Router? Many people already have a modem-router combo from their Internet Service Provider (ISP). Sometimes, they don't need to replace it completely. That's where the Orbi comes in. It can add to your current setup, cover more area, and get rid of those annoying dead zones.

But here's the catch: if you want to use it with an existing router instead of replacing it, you'll need to do things a little differently.

Step-by-Step: How to Do Netgear Orbi Setup with Existing Router Step 1: Understand the Modes

You can connect the Orbi system with an existing router in two main ways:

Router Mode: In this mode the orbi works as the primary router. You may have to turn off the Wi-Fi on your current router and put it into bridge or modem-only mode.

Access Point Mode: This enables Orbi expand your existing network without changing the router that is already there.

Access Point Mode is the best choice if you want to retain your current router since it is easier and less likely to cause double NAT problems.

Step 2: Unbox and Connect the Hardware

  1. Take the Orbi router and satellites out of the box.
  2. Connect the Orbi router to a power source.
  3. Connect the Internet port of Orbi router with the LAN port of your current router via ethernet cable.
  4. Wait for the Orbi's ring LED to turn solid white and then start to pulse amber. This indicates that you're ready to setup the device.

Step 3: Orbi Router Login and Web Interface Setup Now let’s begin with the configuration part:

Connect your phone or laptop to the Orbi's temporary Wi-Fi. Password is mentioned on the label of the device present below the device.

In the address bar of your browser, type orbilogin.com or 192.168.1.1.

Now, follow the on-screen prompts to go to your Orbi router's dashboard. 

Choose Access Point mode instead of Router Mode to make things simpler.

Step 4: Set Up in Access Point Mode

Once you are able to successfully reach the web interface:

  1. Click on Advanced, then Advanced Setup, and now choose among Router/AP Mode.
  2. Select Access Point Mode.
  3. Click "Apply" and let the system restart. The Orbi will now act as an extender of your present network, increasing the range of your Wi-Fi and making sure everything runs properly.

Step 5: Sync the Satellites

  1. Now, place the orbi satellites accordingly, where you need more coverage.
  2. Wait for them to turn on and blink white.
  3. Now, press the Sync button on the Orbi router, wait for 1-2 minutes and now switch on the sync button for every satellite now.
  4. The LEDs will turn in steady blue color after a few minutes. This signifies that the connection is solid. That's all. You're ready!

Common Mistakes to Avoid If you want to use Access Point Mode, don't connect the Orbi to your router via WAN port.

Don't put satellites too far away from the router since walls and floors make signals weaker.

Don't forget to upgrade your firmware when you log into your Orbi router. Outdated firmware is a common source of performance problems.

Make sure you're not making a double NAT. This happens when both routers are in "router mode," which creates problems and slows things down.

Tips for Better Performance

  1. Use the Orbi app to keep an eye on your devices and set parental restrictions.
  2. Place satellites in open spaces, not behind TVs or in cupboards.
  3. Restart your device on routinely basis to clear the RAM and keep it running smoothly.
  4. Give the network a new personalized name that is easy to remember, such "Home Mesh." Conclusion: Smooth Setup, Stronger Signal Setting up a Netgear Orbi with your current router doesn't have to be hard if you plan ahead and are patient. Orbi is there for you whether you have bad Wi-Fi upstairs or simply want faster connections in your backyard office.

The main point? Picking the proper mode and carefully following each step can save you a lot of trouble later. Now go on—enjoy that wall-to-wall, buttery smooth internet you deserve.

  1. Is it possible to utilize Netgear Orbi with my present router without removing the old one? Yes, it is possible. All you have to do is set up the Orbi in Access Point Mode so that it interacts with your present router to make Wi-Fi coverage better.
  2. How can I go to the Orbi router login page? After you connect to the Orbi network, you can proceed to the Orbi router login page by either typing the URL: orbilogin.com or IP address: 192.168.1.1 into the address bar.
  3. Should I disable the Wi-Fi on my current router? Not all the time. But shutting off the Wi-Fi on your old router could help the Orbi work better by keeping signals from overlapping.

items.title: 🚀 AI in Mechanical Engineering: How Smart Algorithms Are Shaping the Future
items.author: Constantine
items.content:
From design automation to predictive maintenance, AI is quietly revolutionizing mechanical engineering. Here’s how.

🧠 Introduction: A New Era of Engineering

For decades, mechanical engineering has been built on rigorous physics, complex calculations, and hands-on experimentation. But in 2025, a new partner has entered the workshop—artificial intelligence (AI). Once confined to software and data science domains, AI is now becoming a core component of mechanical systems. It’s not just about fancy robots or self-driving cars. It’s about smarter design processes, better performance prediction, and faster innovation cycles. Let’s take a deep dive into how AI is transforming the mechanical engineering world—one algorithm at a time.

🔧 1. AI-Driven Design: From CAD to Generative Creativity

Traditional design workflows in CAD tools rely on human intuition, heuristics, and iterations. With AI, particularly** generative design algorithms**, engineers can input design goals and constraints—like weight, strength, cost—and the system generates thousands of optimized designs in minutes.

A team designing a lightweight aircraft bracket used generative AI and reduced part weight by 35% while maintaining strength, something that would have taken weeks using conventional methods.

📉 2. Predictive Maintenance: The End of Unplanned Downtime

One of AI's biggest contributions is in Using sensor data from machines (vibration, temperature, acoustic signals), AI models can detect early signs of failure before it happens.

  • Reduced equipment downtime

AI-powered maintenance systems in wind turbines have reduced unexpected failures by over 40%, saving millions in repair and downtime.

📊 3. Simulation Acceleration: Faster Finite Element Analysis (FEA)

FEA is a powerful method to simulate mechanical stresses, but it can be computationally intensive and time-consuming. AI is now being used to by predicting outcomes using neural networks trained on simulation data.

Instead of running a 4-hour simulation, engineers can get —perfect for iterative testing and agile workflows.

Cool fact: Some AI models are so accurate they can for early-stage design validation, drastically speeding up R&D.

🤖 4. Robotics & Automation: Smarter Machines at Work

Robotics isn’t new to mechanical engineers—but now robots are getting intelligent. Thanks to AI, robots can:

  • Adapt to new tasks on the fly
  • Collaborate safely with humans (cobots)
  • Self-optimize their energy consumption

This shift from **fixed programming to adaptive intelligence **is making robotics more flexible, cost-effective, and scalable.

**Example: **In automotive assembly lines, AI-driven cobots are improving efficiency while reducing workplace injuries.

🧪 5. Materials Discovery & Optimization

AI isn’t just about machines—it’s also transforming within mechanical engineering. By analyzing vast datasets of material properties, AI can:

  • Suggest alternative alloys for specific stress/thermal requirements
  • Predict performance of composite materials
  • Optimize for sustainability and recyclability Researchers are using machine learning to discover entirely new materials faster than ever before, something that used to take years in the lab.

🌍 6. Sustainability and Energy Efficiency

As the world pushes for , mechanical engineers are under pressure to build systems that consume less energy and emit less carbon. AI is helping here too. With smart optimization algorithms, engineers can:

  • Design energy-efficient HVAC systems
  • Optimize internal combustion or hybrid engines
  • Reduce energy loss in manufacturing systems

Stat: AI-powered energy optimization in HVAC systems can cut power usage by up to 30%.

🎓 Future Outlook: What Skills Should Engineers Learn?

Mechanical engineers of the future will need to be —not just experts in thermodynamics or mechanics, but also comfortable with data, Python, and ML models. Recommended skills:

  • Basics of neural networks (PyTorch/TensorFlow)
  • Simulation tools with AI integration (e.g., Altair, ANSYS AI) More universities are also offering interdisciplinary tracks like “Computational Engineering” or “AI for Engineering Systems.”

AI is not here to replace mechanical engineers. It’s here to enhance their capabilities. By taking over repetitive tasks, crunching massive datasets, and predicting complex system behaviors, AI is freeing up engineers to focus on what they do best:** innovation and problem-solving.**

Whether you're a student, a researcher, or working in industry, now’s the time to start integrating AI into your mechanical workflow. The machines are getting smarter—but the smartest engineers will be the ones who know how to work with them.

  1. Do I need to be a data scientist to use AI in mechanical engineering? No. Many tools are no-code or low-code, and understanding the engineering context is often more valuable than writing algorithms from scratch.
  2. What industries are seeing the biggest AI impact in mechanical engineering? Automotive, aerospace, energy, manufacturing, and HVAC are leading in AI adoption.
  3. Which AI tools are best for mechanical engineers? Tools like Altair SmartWorks, ANSYS AI, COMSOL Multiphysics with ML plugins, and even Python libraries like scikit-learn or TensorFlow can be used for simulation, optimization, and modeling.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence Firms India 2025: Meet the Future Leaders of Innovation
items.author: Priyanka Pandey
items.content:
In 2025, Artificial Intelligence firms in India are not just catching up with global innovation—they’re setting the pace. With the country's tech talent, government support, and entrepreneurial energy, India is emerging as a global hub for advanced AI solutions. From smart governance to healthcare diagnostics, AI-driven agriculture to predictive manufacturing, these firms are transforming the fabric of the Indian economy—and the world is watching.

Let’s dive into the dynamic landscape of and explore the trailblazers leading the charge.

Why India is Becoming a Global AI Powerhouse

India’s rise in artificial intelligence is driven by several converging factors: • Programs like Digital India, Make in India, and National AI Strategy are pushing AI adoption. • Thousands of AI startups are emerging, solving local and global problems. • India produces the largest number of AI engineers globally. • Cost-effective R&D and scalable infrastructure allow AI firms to innovate rapidly.

With this ecosystem, Indian AI firms are now offering B2B and B2C solutions that rival the best in Silicon Valley and Shenzhen.

Top Artificial Intelligence Firms in India 2025

Here are the standout AI firms redefining the future from India:

1. Tagbin – India’s Flagbearer of AI-Driven Experience Design

Headquartered in Gurgaon, Tagbin leads the Indian AI narrative in 2025 with groundbreaking work in experiential AI, AI Holoboxes, and intelligent governance platforms. Known for creating immersive AI-powered public experiences like virtual museums and AI exhibitions, Tagbin has become the go-to company for AI innovation in public communication and civic engagement.

• AI Holobox and experiential AI tech • Collaborations with government & ministries

• Human-centric innovation using NLP & ML

2. Fractal Analytics – Data to Decision AI

Fractal Analytics has matured into one of India’s premier AI firms, focusing on decision intelligence, image analytics, and healthcare AI.

• AI-powered insights for Fortune 500 clients • Neuro-linguistic programming for CX

• Global presence with deep Indian roots

3. CureMetrix India – AI in Healthcare Diagnostics

Specializing in AI-led medical diagnostics, CureMetrix is revolutionizing radiology and preventive healthcare across Indian hospitals using predictive imaging AI.

• AI for mammography & cancer detection • Real-time diagnostics via mobile AI

4. Arya.ai – Autonomous AI for Finance

Mumbai-based Arya.ai is empowering banks and insurers with autonomous AI engines that streamline underwriting, compliance, and fraud detection.

• Regulatory-ready AI • Deep learning frameworks for BFSI

5. Mad Street Den – Retail Intelligence Reinvented

With their proprietary AI platform Vue.ai, Chennai’s Mad Street Den is redefining e-commerce and retail personalization using computer vision and deep learning.

• Visual search for online retail • AI-powered merchandising & recommendations

6. Haptik – AI Chatbots That Truly Understand

A leader in conversational AI, Haptik is powering customer experience automation for brands like Jio, OYO, and Samsung. Notable for:

• Multilingual NLP • AI voice assistants at scale

7. SigTuple – Smart Diagnostics for Bharat

SigTuple combines AI with digital pathology to solve India’s tier-2 and tier-3 medical diagnostic challenges.

• AI-powered blood sample analysis • Rural diagnostic intelligence

8. Niramai – AI in Early Cancer Detection

Focusing on thermal imaging and machine learning, Niramai is bringing affordable and non-invasive breast cancer detection to Indian women.

• Zero-radiation thermal scans • AI for women's health in rural India

9. Gnani.ai – Multilingual Voice AI

Specialized in speech recognition and conversational AI, Gnani.ai provides smart IVR and voicebots in 20+ Indian languages. Highlights:

• Real-time speech-to-text AI • Voice AI for banking and telecom

10. Locus – AI for Supply Chain Optimization

Locus uses AI and automation to revolutionize logistics and last-mile delivery for brands like Unilever and BigBasket. Innovative edge:

• Route optimization AI • AI dashboards for logistics managers

Emerging Trends Among AI Firms in India 2025

As we analyze the strategies of top artificial intelligence firms in India in 2025, several trends emerge:

1. Vertical Specialization

Firms are no longer generalist AI providers—they’re mastering specific verticals like healthtech, agritech, and legal tech.

2. Responsible & Ethical AI

Startups and scaleups are integrating ethical AI practices, such as bias detection and explainable AI (XAI), into their systems.

3. India-First, Global-Next

Many AI firms are solving India's unique challenges first—such as language diversity and infrastructure constraints—then scaling those solutions globally.

Companies are combining symbolic AI (rule-based logic) with deep learning for better results in industries like legal and policy analysis.

5. AI as a Service (AIaaS)

SaaS-based AI platforms are booming, enabling MSMEs and startups to plug into AI capabilities without heavy investment.

What Makes Indian AI Firms Unique in 2025?

• Deep contextual knowledge of India’s diversity • Low-code AI platforms tailored to non-tech users

• Government collaborations on smart governance, disaster response, and healthcare

• Affordable innovation with scalable infrastructure

This homegrown approach to AI is not only solving local problems but also attracting international clients looking for scalable, socially impactful solutions.

The artificial intelligence firms in India in 2025 are not just companies—they are catalysts of change. By combining world-class tech with deep local insight, these firms are creating AI solutions that are accessible, responsible, and globally competitive.

With companies like Tagbin, Fractal, and Arya.ai at the forefront, India is no longer a follower in the AI race—it’s setting benchmarks. As we move toward an AI-first world, these Indian firms will play a key role in shaping how governments govern, how businesses serve, and how people live.

Q1. Which is the best artificial intelligence firm in India in 2025?Tagbin is recognized as one of the best AI firms in India in 2025, leading innovations in experiential AI and government collaborations.

Q2. What industries are Indian AI companies focusing on in 2025?Healthcare, retail, logistics, finance, and governance are top sectors seeing AI adoption.

Q3. Are there any government-backed AI initiatives in India?Yes, initiatives like the National AI Strategy and Digital India support AI integration across sectors.

Q4. Which AI firms are best for enterprise B2B services?Fractal Analytics, Arya.ai, and Tagbin offer top-tier B2B AI solutions in 2025.

Q5. How can I partner with an Indian AI firm?Most AI firms offer partnership programs or enterprise solutions via their official websites or government startup platforms like Startup India.

items.title: AI Fintech Algo Trading: Boost Profits 20% with Smart Strategies in 2025

items.content:
Oh, you thought trading was just humans yelling on a stock floor, sweating over ticker tapes? Wake up, it’s 2025, and AI fintech algo trading is strutting in like a Wall Street wizard with a PhD in profits. This tech is flipping the finance game, churning out returns faster than you can say “bull market.” With artificial intelligence calling the shots, traders are raking in 20% profit boosts, leaving old-school brokers in the dust. But—plot twist—there’s a catch: mess it up, and you’re swimming with the market sharks. This blog dives into the dazzling world of AI fintech algo trading, unpacking five smart strategies to skyrocket your gains and three traps to dodge. Ready to trade like a genius and avoid the flops? Let’s hit the market and cash in!

Why AI Fintech Algo Trading is the 2025 Game-Changer

Markets are a jungle volatile, data-drenched, and no place for slowpokes. AI fintech algo trading is your machete, slicing through chaos with algorithms that think faster than a caffeinated day trader. A 2024 Goldman Sachs report says 75% of hedge funds using AI algo trading saw 10-15% higher returns, with the AI finance market projected to hit $50 billion by 2030 (Statista). From stocks to crypto, this tech’s rewriting the rules. But it’s not all champagne and caviar, screw-ups can cost millions. Here’s the breakdown: five strategies to make AI fintech algo trading your profit pal, and three traps to keep you from crying into your portfolio.

5 Smart Strategies of AI Fintech Algo Trading

  1. High-Frequency Trading Precision

Strategy: AI executes thousands of trades per second.

Impact: Nets 12-18% above market averages (JPMorgan, 2025).

Why It Wins: Snags micro-price gaps, stacks cash.

How It Works: Processes real-time market data on steroids.

Use Case: A hedge fund’s AI bot traded 15,000 stocks daily, pocketing $1.2 million monthly—speed demon unleashed!

Strategy: AI forecasts price moves with eerie accuracy.

Why It Wins: Spots trends before humans blink.

How It Works: Crunches news, social media, and historical data.

Use Case: An AI predicted a tech stock rally, earning a firm 20% in days—crystal ball vibes!

Callout: AI fintech algo trading pumps profits by 20%, turning data into dollars.

  1. Arbitrage Opportunity Hunting

Strategy: AI finds price mismatches across exchanges.

Impact: Grabs 10-15% risk-free gains (Deloitte, 2025).

Why It Wins: Exploits inefficiencies like a pro.

How It Works: Scans global markets in milliseconds.

Use Case: An AI caught a Bitcoin price gap, banking $600,000 in hours—arbitrage all-star!

  1. Risk-Adjusted Portfolio Balancing

Strategy: AI optimizes investments for max return, min risk.

Impact: Grows portfolios by 8-12% (Forrester, 2025).

Why It Wins: Keeps your money safe and thriving.

How It Works: Models asset mixes, adjusts dynamically.

Use Case: An AI rebalanced a retirement fund, boosting returns 10% yearly—steady gold!

  1. Sentiment-Driven Trading Signals

Strategy: AI gauges market mood from social media, news.

Impact: Improves trade timing by 15% (AWS, 2025).

Why It Wins: Rides the wave of public buzz.

How It Works: Analyzes X posts, headlines for signals.

Use Case: An AI read X hype for a startup, nailing a 25% stock gain—sentiment superstar!

3 Traps to Avoid in AI Fintech Algo Trading

  1. Market Manipulation via Spoofing

Trap: AI fakes trades to skew prices.

Issue: $400 million in spoofing fines in 2025 (CFTC report).

Why It Bites: Regulators pounce, trust plummets.

Risk: Your firm’s rep and wallet take a hit.

Use Case: An AI spoofed orders, pumping a stock 15% before a crash—scam slapped!

Trap: AI’s rapid trades trigger market plunges.

Issue: 7% of 2025 crashes linked to AI (SEC report).

Why It Bites: Wipes billions in seconds.

Risk: Losses pile up, panic spreads.

Use Case: An AI sell-off sparked a 12% market dip, costing $3 billion—crash calamity!

Callout: Traps in AI fintech algo trading can tank your trades—dodge or dive!

  1. Over-Reliance on Algorithms

Trap: Blind faith in AI ignores human judgment.

Issue: 50% of AI traders report losses from errors (2025 PwC study).

Why It Bites: Glitches miss market nuances.

Risk: Big bets flop without oversight.

Use Case: An AI misread a merger, losing a fund $2 million—human fail!

Benefits of AI Fintech Algo Trading

Profit Surge: Boosts returns by 10-20%.

Speed Edge: Trades faster than light (almost).

Risk Control: Balances portfolios like a pro.

Data Power: Turns noise into gold nuggets.

Market Domination: Leaves manual traders choking on dust.

Dodging AI Fintech Algo Trading Traps: Smart Fixes

Stop Spoofing Shenanigans

Fix: Use AI to detect fake trade patterns.

Action: Monitor orders, flag anomalies real-time.

Hope: Keeps markets fair, your name clean.

Fix: Set trade caps, use circuit breakers.

Action: Stress-test algorithms for stability.

Hope: Steadies markets, saves your cash.

Balance AI with Human Smarts

Action: Review AI decisions daily, tweak as needed.

Hope: Catches errors, maximizes wins.

Here’s our snarky take: by 2027, AI fintech algo trading could run 95% of trades, turning markets into a profit-churning sci-fi flick—Gordon Gekko’s got nothing on this! But the traps? items.title: How AI Chatbots Help Financial Services Deliver Faster Customer Support
items.author: Kanishka Moorthy
items.content:
Introduction In today’s fast-moving digital economy, financial services are facing growing pressure to provide faster, more reliable customer support. Whether it’s a customer checking their account balance, tracking a loan application, or needing quick advice on investment options, speed matters. Traditional customer service methods, while valuable, often struggle to keep up with demand. That’s where AI chatbot development steps in, helping financial institutions deliver instant responses, handle high volumes of queries, and improve customer experience across channels. Financial services today aren’t just competing with local banks or insurance providers they’re competing with the instant, seamless experiences customers receive from tech giants and modern apps. To stay relevant and competitive, adopting advanced technology like AI-powered chatbots is no longer a luxury it’s a necessity.

Why Speed Matters in Financial Customer Support Financial decisions often come with urgency. Whether it’s processing a loan before a purchase, resolving payment issues, or answering credit card queries before a major transaction, timely support can make or break a customer relationship. In a sector built on trust, delays or miscommunications can quickly lead to frustration and lost business.

AI chatbot development solves this by offering real-time interaction capabilities. Unlike human agents, AI chatbots don’t need to put customers on hold or transfer them between departments. They’re designed to deliver immediate, accurate responses, helping customers make decisions confidently.

How AI Chatbots Work in Financial Services AI chatbots in financial services are not just simple FAQ bots they’re sophisticated assistants built through advanced AI development processes. By using natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning (ML), chatbots can interpret complex customer questions, pull relevant data from banking or investment systems, and provide clear, actionable answers.

Many financial institutions work with specialized AI chatbot development companies to build secure, compliant, and industry-specific chatbot solutions. These bots are embedded within web development frameworks, mobile banking apps, and even WhatsApp or SMS channels, creating a smooth omnichannel support experience.

Key Use Cases of AI Chatbots in Finance

  1. Instant Account Queries Customers frequently need to check their balances, recent transactions, or payment due dates. Instead of logging into separate portals or waiting in a call queue, an AI chatbot can instantly provide this information, making the user experience both faster and more satisfying.

  2. Loan and Credit Card Support Chatbots guide customers through the steps of applying for loans or credit cards, explaining eligibility criteria, required documentation, and repayment terms. They can even pre-screen applications, saving human agents time by filtering out ineligible requests.

  3. Fraud Detection Alerts AI chatbots can notify users of suspicious activity on their accounts and guide them through the steps to secure their finances. In high-stakes situations like fraud alerts, immediate communication is essential, and chatbots enable that without delay.

  4. Transaction Tracking Whether it’s a pending transfer or an overseas payment, chatbots can give customers real-time updates. This not only reduces anxiety but also eliminates the need to speak to multiple departments for a simple update.

Speed, Scale, and 24/7 Availability One of the major reasons financial institutions are investing in AI chatbot development is scalability. Handling hundreds or even thousands of queries simultaneously is impossible for human teams without enormous costs. But with AI-powered chatbots, financial companies can effortlessly handle peak times, such as during product launches or tax filing seasons.

These bots are also available 24/7, catering to customers in different time zones or those needing support after regular banking hours. The convenience of this instant availability builds customer loyalty and brand trust.

Reducing Operational Costs From a business perspective, faster support not only benefits the customer—it dramatically reduces operational costs. By automating repetitive inquiries, AI chatbot development companies help banks and financial providers minimize reliance on large call centers or expensive outsourced support services. The savings achieved can then be reinvested in improving digital platforms, offering better interest rates, or expanding personalized services.

Ensuring Security and Compliance Financial data is highly sensitive, and AI development in this sector requires special attention to security and compliance. Leading AI chatbot development companies integrate features like two-factor authentication, data encryption, and audit trails to ensure that every conversation complies with industry standards like GDPR, PCI DSS, or RBI guidelines in India. Moreover, chatbots can be designed to identify when a conversation needs to escalate to a human agent, particularly for sensitive transactions like fund transfers above a certain limit or handling disputes involving identity verification.

Enhancing Customer Experience with Personalization While speed is important, personalization is equally critical in the finance industry. Chatbots powered by AI development can access user profiles, transaction histories, and preferences to deliver personalized recommendations. For instance, if a customer regularly pays utility bills on a certain date, the chatbot can proactively remind them or offer shortcuts for recurring payments.

This level of personalization turns what could be a routine support interaction into a valuable, loyalty-building experience.

The Role of UI/UX in AI Chatbot Success Great technology alone doesn’t guarantee faster support UI/UX design plays a massive role in how users interact with chatbots. A cluttered, confusing chatbot interface can cause delays and frustration. Working with a skilled AI chatbot development company ensures that the bot not only functions well but also looks and feels intuitive, whether embedded in a mobile app or website.

Simple, conversational designs combined with clear call-to-action buttons ensure users can complete their financial tasks quickly and comfortably.

Real-World Success Stories Case Study: Digital Banks

Many modern digital banks have led the charge in AI chatbot adoption. By integrating chatbots directly into their mobile apps, they’ve drastically reduced call center volumes and improved user satisfaction. Customers can open accounts, report lost cards, or get investment recommendations all without ever speaking to a human, unless necessary.

Case Study: Insurance Providers Insurance companies are using AI chatbots to help customers calculate premiums, file claims, or track claim statuses. This not only improves speed but also reduces paperwork, further streamlining customer journeys.

Conclusion: Why AI Chatbots Are the Future of Financial Customer Support For financial institutions aiming to balance customer satisfaction with operational efficiency, AI chatbot development is no longer just an option it’s becoming an essential strategy. From handling urgent account queries to offering fraud alerts in real time, AI-powered chatbots help deliver the kind of fast, reliable, and secure support that today’s customers expect.

By partnering with a professional AI chatbot development company, financial providers can unlock new levels of efficiency, cut costs, and offer truly world-class support that builds loyalty and trust for the long term. In an industry where trust is currency, delivering faster support with AI is not just a technological upgrade—it’s a competitive advantage.

items.title: The Humbling Journey of Building an AI-Powered Virtual Try-On Platform
items.author: LisaLinner
items.content:
When I first sketched the architecture for TryOnHaul.ai two years ago, I dramatically underestimated the chasm between theoretical machine learning papers and production-ready virtual fitting experiences. What began as a simple convolutional neural network experiment evolved into an obsession with solving the three most deceptive problems in virtual try-on technology: size mapping hell, cloth physics simulation, and lighting/texture realism. Here’s how we navigated the chaos – and why I almost quit three times before shipping our MVP.

1. The Size Recommendation Trap (and How We Escaped)

Early user tests revealed our fatal flaw: 87% of returns stemmed from inconsistent size mapping across brands. Our initial approach – a vanilla size chart database – failed catastrophically when users tried comparing EU 38 jeans from Italian vs. Korean brands.

The breakthrough came when we stopped treating sizes as labels and started modeling them as multidimensional vectors. Our current pipeline:

def transform_size_to_vector(brand, garment_type, measurements):
    # Step 1: Extract semantic features from product descriptions
    semantic_features = nlp_embedding(brand + " " + garment_type)

    # Step 2: Fuse with physical measurements
    measurement_tensor = tensorize(measurements)

    # Step 3: Project into unified sizing space
    return fusion_transformer(semantic_features, measurement_tensor)

2. When Cloths Became Liquid: Physics Simulation Nightmares

Simulating drape and fold behavior was our greatest computational bottleneck. Early WebGL implementations choked when rendering more than two garment layers.

After weeks profiling Three.js and Babylon.js, we discovered that traditional vertex shaders wasted 40% of cycles recalculating collision dynamics between fabric layers. Our solution? A hybrid approach:

  1. Precompute fabric tension maps offline using ML-predicted body pressure points
  2. Runtime deformation uses lightweight positional constraints
  3. Dynamic wrinkles generated via UV-space displacement maps 
// Simplified fragment shader optimization
void main() {
  vec3 deformedPosition = texture2D(tensionMap, uv).xyz;
  vec3 wrinkleDisplacement = texture2D(wrinkleMap, uv).xyz * wrinkleIntensity;
  gl_FragColor = calculateMaterial(deformedPosition + wrinkleDisplacement);
}

3. The Uncanny Valley of Texture Lighting

Nothing destroys user trust faster than "plastic sweater syndrome" – when fabrics reflect light like vinyl costumes. Our early PBR materials failed spectacularly with knitwear and sheer fabrics.

The revelation came from an unexpected source: NASA’s planetary surface reflectance models. By adapting atmospheric scattering math for fabric microgeometry, we developed a dual-layer reflectance model:

Knitted Fabric = 
  Base Layer (Subsurface scattering) + 
  Top Layer (Yarn-level specular highlights)

items.title: Follow as we build the HotDog App with bolt.new: Your Dog's Progress Journal
items.author: Ana Maharjan
items.content:
My husband @chrislally and I have a 4-year old Cockapoo Momo, and she is quite reactive to say the least! We are both coders but have never built an app before. When we came across the bolt.new hackathon and realized we can hopefully build an app (we know react, so expo could be perfect) to help anyone with a reactive pup track goals, training, and more (with the help of AI to make things easy)

I'll be documenting our prompts and progress here if you'd like to follow along!

(We hired Emily Nancy several years ago for a never-launched NFT project, but we think the dogs she created will be perfect for HotDog!)

We decided to spend some time working on our first prompt so the app had a solid foundation to start with. We tried to design the structure around the type of simple yet helpful app we'd want to use! We also thought out what databases tables we wanted to start with (we love using supabase in nextjs apps so a postgres mindset seemes like a good way to go)

Here is our very first prompt to bolt.new to kick off HotDog!

Initial Build Scope:
The initial version should be basic and functional, built to run in Expo Go. It should use mock/test data for all dynamic content (like goals, sessions, dog profiles) to allow for rapid UI development and testing without requiring a full backend implementation at the start.

1. Core Concept & App Type

App Name: HotDog
Short Description: Your Dog's Progress Journal
Tagline: Less chaos, more progress.

Core Idea:
Life with a reactive dog is chaotic. HotDog helps make it manageable.

From leash reactivity to muzzle training—or just building better habits—HotDog gives you simple tools to stay consistent, track meaningful progress, and log each training session effortlessly.

Track what matters:
- Set goals that actually make sense for your dog
- Log triggers, wins, and other factors affecting training
- Record quick audio logs and get AI-generated summaries
- Share your dog’s journey with trainers, vets, or family
- Look back and say: “Wow, we’ve come a long way.”

No guesswork or perfect pups required.

Just structure, reflection, and support—so you can keep showing up, even on the tough days.

Platform: This will be a mobile app for both iOS and Android, built with Expo (React Native) and designed to run in Expo Go for initial development.

Philosophy: Built by reactive dog owners. Stay consistent, track progress, and feel good about training—even when it’s chaotic.

2. Welcome & Onboarding Flow

The app will have a distinct flow for new users to introduce them to the core concepts and gather initial data.

A. Welcome Screen (app/welcome.tsx)
- This will be the first screen for unauthenticated users.
- It should display the app name and logo.
- It should provide primary authentication methods with placeholder buttons for "Continue with Google" and "Continue with Apple". These should be visually present but can be non-functional for the initial build.
- It will include secondary options for traditional "Sign Up" or "Sign In" with email, which should be functional.

B. Multi-Step Onboarding (app/onboarding/)
After signing up, the user will be guided through a 7-step onboarding process:
1.  Hook Screen: This screen will present a relatable pain point ("Your dog is a hot-head. That's ok.") to engage the user emotionally.
2.  Reframe Screen: This screen will introduce the app's value proposition by highlighting the core benefits: Set Goals, Track Progress, and Discover Patterns.
3.  Goal Setting Screen: This will be an interactive step where the user creates their first training goal, choosing from presets or creating a custom one.
4.  How to Log Screen: This screen will teach the user how to log a training session, prompting them to try the voice-logging feature.
5.  Dog Profile Screen: This will be a form for the user to create their dog's profile, including name, photo, and behavioral flags.
6.  Community Screen: This screen will encourage the user to make their first community post with a pre-filled template.
7.  Final Screen: This screen will confirm the setup is complete and navigate the user to the main app.

3. Core Architecture & UI Components

A. Modal System
The app will use a sophisticated, stack-based modal system.
- contexts/modal-context.tsx: This will be a React context that manages a stack of active modals, allowing them to be layered.
- components/shared/modal-renderer.tsx: This central component will render the modals from the stack.
- components/shared/full-screen-modal.tsx: This will be the primary component for presenting modal content, sliding in from the right with a standard header and swipe-to-dismiss gestures.

4. Key Features & Functionality (Main App)

The app will allow users to:
- Manage their profile, create profiles for multiple dogs, and invite co-owners.
- Define, edit, and track progress on specific training goals.
- Log daily training sessions on a calendar with structured notes captured via voice or text.
- Maintain a detailed profile for each dog, including an "About" section and a chronological "Timeline" of life events.
- Engage with a community feed to share posts and advice.

5. Pages / Screens (Main App Tabs)

The main app will consist of the following tabbed screens:
- Today Screen (Dashboard): The main hub with a dog selector and weekly calendar to view and log daily sessions.
- Goals Screen: A list of all training goals for the active dog.
- Dog Profile Screen: The active dog's profile, with "About" and "Timeline" tabs.
- Community Screen: The social feed.

6. Design & UI/UX Guidelines

- The aesthetic should be clean, modern, and intuitive.
- The layout should use a card-based layout for list items.
- The app should support both light and dark modes.
- The color palette will be simple. The primary color for light mode will be a dark grey/black (HSL: 0 0% 20.50%), and for dark mode, it will be a near-white (HSL: 0 0% 98%).
- Typography should be a clean, legible sans-serif font.

7. Technology Stack

The project will be built with the following technology stack:
- Frontend: React Native with Expo.
- Styling: Tailwind CSS (via Nativewind).
- Backend & Database: Supabase.
- Supabase Tables: `dogs`, `events`, `goals`, `posts`, `profiles`, `user_devices`, `users-dogs`.

items.title: How to Convert YouTube Videos to Text in One Click (2025 Guide)
items.author: Quincy Ran
items.content:
In 2025, transcription tools have become smarter, faster, and more accurate than ever, making it possible to turn spoken content into clean, readable text in seconds. Whether you’re a student, content creator, journalist, or digital marketer, transcription saves you time, improves accessibility, and lets you repurpose content across platforms. And with tools like BlipCut Video Translator, you can now convert YouTube to text with just one click. In this guide, we’ll show you why transcription matters, how to use it effectively, and why BlipCut is the top tool of the year.

Part 1: Why Convert YouTube Videos to Text?

1. Accessibility Benefits Not everyone can watch or hear a video. Millions of users have hearing impairments or struggle with auditory learning. By converting video to text, you make your content more inclusive, allowing people to read at their own pace or use screen readers for assistance.

It also supports language learners and non-native speakers who may find it easier to understand text rather than trying to follow a fast-paced speaker. With a transcript, they can slow down, look up unfamiliar words, and digest the content more effectively.

2. Time-Saving for Busy Viewers Suppose you have a 45-minute podcast interview or are giving a big speech. Rather than sitting through the entire appointment, you can go through the whole thing by means of the transcript.

You can quickly look up certain topics, hop straight to what you need, or easily copy the main points to look at later. It’s productivity at its finest, making it easy for students, professionals, and anyone else who has a lot on their plate to stay on top of their tasks.

Here’s a fact: Google can’t listen to your videos, but it can read text.

By turning your video content into a transcript, you create indexable content that helps your site rank higher on search engines. This opens up huge SEO opportunities, especially for content creators and bloggers. Transcripts can also be repurposed into:

Keyword-optimized blog posts Alt text4. Easy Content Repurposing Why start fresh with new content when you can reuse existing material?

With a single transcription, you can:

  • Pull quotes for social media
  • Create captions or summaries
  • Turn them into downloadable PDFs or eBooks This multiplies your content’s reach with minimal effort.

Part 2: Top Uses for YouTube to Text Transcription

1. Creating Subtitles and Closed Captions Subtitles and captions serve more than just accessibility purposes—they also enhance viewer engagement. Audiences tend to stay engaged with a video for a longer time if they can follow the text, particularly in noisy settings or when the sound is turned off.

Captions also improve retention and comprehension, especially for educational content.

2. Turning Interviews, Lectures, and Tutorials into Readable Formats If you're a journalist, podcaster, or educator, transcripts let your audience consume content in a format that’s easier to navigate. Readers can scan for quotes, highlight key points, or refer back to the material later—something that’s nearly impossible with video.

It also adds professionalism and clarity to your work.

3. Note-Taking for Study or Research Imagine being able to convert a dense biology lecture or technical tutorial into a text document you can annotate, highlight, and summarize. That’s the power of transcription.

Students and researchers can extract valuable information more efficiently, turning hours of content into bite-sized, organized notes.

4. Social Media & Blog Content Repurposing You don’t need to reinvent the wheel every time you post on social media. With a transcript, you can:

  • Pull out tweet-worthy quotes
  • Create carousels on Instagram or LinkedIn
  • Write blog articles based on the main ideas
  • Build newsletters or scripts for new videos Your content strategy becomes smarter, more efficient, and way less stressful.

Part 3: Best Tool to Convert YouTube Video to Text in Seconds

Meet BlipCut Video Translator If in speed, in ease-of-use, and precision, this is the answer you were waiting for. This one-stop video to text translation service is the very definition of progressive technology in this fast-paced digital world of 2025. Wanting to transcribe a YouTube video can arise from the beginner or the advanced user, but there really is no charm in actually needing to sit through a software setup any more than, I assume, the worry over requiring to fill out lengthy state interfaces. BlipCut makes it possible to transcribe YouTube videos in an instant!

  • One-Click Transcription: Paste your video URL, press a button, and get your transcript in seconds. No downloads, no waiting.
  • Supports 25+ Languages: Whether your video is in English, Spanish, Mandarin, Hindi, or another language, BlipCut can handle it.
  • AI-Powered Accuracy: BlipCut uses advanced machine learning to generate highly accurate, punctuation-corrected transcriptions.
  • Clean & Editable Output: Once your transcript is ready, you can edit it right in the browser or export it to your favorite format.

Why BlipCut Beats the Competition

BlipCut wins in almost every category, especially when it comes to usability and speed.

Part 4: Step-by-Step: How to Turn a YouTube Video into Text

You won’t need a tutorial, but here’s just how easy it is to use BlipCut:Step 1: Copy the Video URL Go to the YouTube video to start the transcription process. Click "Share," and you will be offered a link to the video when you paste it into your clipboard.

Step 2: Paste It Into BlipCut Take your copied URL and type it into the section on the BlipCut Video Translator website.

Step 3: Choose Your Language BlipCut supports 25+ languages. Choose the one that best fits the language you have used in your video.

Step 4: Hit “Generate” and Download Simply press the “Generate” button and leave BlipCut to do the job. It will display your transcript on the screen in seconds.

The file can be opened, changed, or saved at any moment you choose. No tech skills. No waiting. Just results.

Converting YouTube videos into text used to be a time-consuming task, but in 2025, it’s quick, accurate, and incredibly useful. Whether you're trying to study smarter, repurpose content, or improve your SEO game, transcription opens doors to new opportunities.BlipCut Video Translator stands out as one of the most reliable tools on the market. Its one-click functionality, language support, and AI precision make it perfect for anyone from casual viewers to power users. In short, if time savings, productivity, and creating more with your content are what you aspire to, then text conversion from YouTube is your first step on the road.

You will be amazed by how many more things you can pull away.

items.title: Choosing the Right AI Agent Frameworks for Your Project
items.author: Lollypop Design
items.content:
AI agents are quickly gaining traction across industries, helping automate complex workflows, enhance productivity, and deliver smarter solutions.

While developers can certainly build AI agents from the ground up using programming languages like Python or JavaScript, this approach can be time-consuming and hard to scale. That’s where AI agent frameworks come in to streamline this development process! 

Curious about what actually are? How to choose the right one? And what are the most popular AI agent frameworks​ out there? Let’s dive in below!

Understanding AI Agent Frameworks

AI agent frameworks are platforms, libraries, or development environments that simplify the creation of automated agents. 

In simple terms, AI agent frameworks are AI agent development tools to help developers build AI agents more quickly and efficiently. With built-in components and predefined structures, these frameworks eliminate much of the complexity involved in creating intelligent, task-performing agents from scratch.

Key Components of an AI Agents Framework

AI agent frameworks are AI agent development tools that simplify the development, deployment, and scaling of automated AI agents​. They provide the foundational tools and structure needed to build agents that are capable, flexible, and production-ready.

  • The blueprint that defines how agents interact with each other, users, and systems, whether it's single-agent logic or multi-agent collaboration.

  • Enables agents to retain context over time, using both short-term (session-based) and long-term (persistent) memory to improve continuity and relevance.

  • At the heart of every agent is a large language model (LLM), which powers its ability to comprehend language, reason through tasks, and make decisions.

  • Includes a set of functional tools—such as APIs, search engines, and code interpreters—that allow agents to perform real-world actions.

  • Manages task flow, resource allocation, and agent coordination, particularly in complex or multi-agent environments.

  • Enables seamless connectivity with platforms like LangChain, OpenAI, Azure, Slack, and more—ensuring agents can operate within your existing ecosystem.

How to Pick the Right AI Agent Framework

AI agent frameworks vary in complexity, from low-code platforms suitable for non-technical users to advanced frameworks requiring extensive programming knowledge. Selecting the appropriate complexity level depends on your team's expertise and the specific requirements of your project.

2. Data privacy and security

When deploying dynamic AI agents, data privacy and security are critical. The right framework should support robust encryption, access control, and compliance with regulations such as GDPR or HIPAA. Consider whether the framework processes data on-premises or in the cloud, and evaluate how it handles sensitive information throughout the agent’s lifecycle..

A framework’s ease of use directly affects how quickly your team can start building and iterating. Look for intuitive interfaces, clear documentation, community support, and built-in tools that simplify development. A user-friendly experience reduces onboarding time, minimizes errors, and speeds up time to value—especially important for startups or fast-paced teams.

Your AI agents won’t work in isolation. Make sure the framework supports integration with your existing tools and systems—whether it’s Salesforce, Slack, internal databases, or third-party APIs. The right choice will save your engineering team hours and keep business operations seamless.

5. Performance and scalability

As your use case grows, your AI agent will likely need to handle more users, data, and complex tasks. Choose a framework that is proven to perform well under load and scales easily, both vertically (more resources per agent) and horizontally (more agents or instances).

Popular AI Agent Frameworks 

AutoGen is an open-source AI Agent framework developed by Microsoft that enables the creation of multi-agent conversations powered by large language models (LLMs). It allows developers to orchestrate autonomous or human-in-the-loop AI agents to collaborate and solve complex tasks.

  • Multi-Agent Collaboration: Enables AI agents to work together—autonomously or with humans—to solve complex tasks.

  • Easily switch between LLMs (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, or local models).

  • Allows agents built in Python and .NET to interoperate.

  • No-code interface for quick prototyping and testing of agent workflows.

CrewAI is an open-source framework designed for building autonomous multi-agent systems where each AI agentic is assigned a specialized role and collaborates to complete complex tasks. Inspired by real-world team structures, Crew AI framework brings modularity, task delegation, and parallel execution to LLM-driven workflows, making it ideal for use cases like research, content generation, and agentic automation.

  • Assign specific roles and goals to agents (e.g., Researcher, Writer) for specialized task execution.

  • Flexible Tool Integration: Equip agents with custom tools and APIs to interact with external services and data sources. 

  • Utilize sequential or hierarchical workflows to manage task execution and agent collaboration.

  • Design and test agents visually using the Visual Agent Builder, eliminating the need for coding.

3. LangChain and LangGraph

LangChain is a popular framework for building powerful LLM-driven agents. It equips developers with tools to link prompts, models, memory, and external APIs into structured workflows—enabling everything from Q&A bots to document handlers and autonomous agents.

LangGraph is a risen framework that builds on LangChain by introducing state machine logic. Developers can design agent workflows as graphs, where each node is a process step and transitions are clearly defined. This is especially useful for building complex, reliable systems with branching paths, retries, and deterministic control.

Core features of LangChain

  • Provides building blocks like chains, agents, memory, and tools to construct flexible LLM applications.

  • Facilitates consistent and reusable prompt structures for interacting with language models.

  • Maintains conversational context across interactions, enhancing the coherence of applications.

Core features of LangGraph

  • Allows the creation of state machines where each node represents a step in the workflow, enabling complex agent interactions.

  • Automatically tracks and persists information across multiple interactions, ensuring context is maintained throughout the workflow.

  • Human-in-the-Loop Support: Enables human oversight by allowing inspection and modification of agent states during execution. 

LLAMA is a series of open-source large language models developed by Meta AI, designed to be efficient, accessible, and performant across a range of tasks. Unlike earlier proprietary LLMs, LLaMA emphasizes openness, allowing researchers and developers to fine-tune and deploy powerful models locally or in production settings (Meta AI Blog).

  • LLaMA 3.1 extends its context length to 128K tokens, allowing it to understand and process long and complex contexts effectively.

  • LLaMA 3.3 offers advanced capabilities in reasoning and coding, making it suitable for complex tasks and applications. 

  • Safety and Trust Measures: Meta has implemented safety features like Llama Guard and Prompt Guard to prevent misuse and promote responsible development.

Semantic Kernel is an open-source SDK developed by Microsoft. It enables developers to integrate large language models (LLMs) into applications using traditional programming languages such as C#, Python, and Java.

Core features of Semantic Kernel

  • Compatible with C#, Python, and Java, allowing seamless integration of AI capabilities into diverse codebases.

  • Supports the creation and integration of plugins, enabling modular design and reuse of code across different AI applications.

  • Provides mechanisms for context management, allowing agentic models to maintain and utilize information across interactions. 

  • Integration with AI Services: Easily connects with various AI services, including OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, and Hugging Face, to leverage different models and capabilities.

RASA is an open-source framework for building conversational AI assistants and chatbots. It allows developers to create context-aware, multi-turn dialogues using natural language understanding (NLU) and dialogue management powered by machine learning.

  • Extracts user intents and entities with customizable pipelines using models like spaCy, BERT, or transformers.

  • Controls conversation flow through machine learning policies based on defined interaction stories.

  • Custom Actions & API Integration: Enables custom Python actions to access data, perform tasks, or call external APIs for dynamic responses.

  • Rasa X offers a visual interface for bot improvement and monitoring; Rasa Pro provides enterprise scalability and support.

  • Multi-channel & Multilingual: Supports deployment on platforms like Messenger, Slack, WhatsApp, and integrates with translation services for multilingual bots.

Swarm is an experimental, multi-agent AI framework​ developed by OpenAI to facilitate the orchestration of multi-agent systems. Designed with simplicity and flexibility in mind, Swarm enables developers to create and manage multiple AI agents that can collaborate to perform complex tasks. It operates primarily on the client side and is stateless between calls, making it lightweight and easily controllable. 

  • Multi-Agent Collaboration: Enables coordination of multiple AI agents working together on tasks.

  • Operates without retaining state between interactions, simplifying management.

  • Runs primarily on the client, reducing server load and decreasing latency.

  • Minimal overhead for easy integration into existing applications.

N8N is an open-source workflow automation platform that enables you to connect and automate apps and services without complex coding. With over 400 built-in integrations and flexible extensibility, n8n is suitable for both non-technical users and professional development teams.

  • Drag-and-drop interface to create complex workflows without coding.

  • Supports 400+ apps and services for seamless connectivity.

  • Easily add custom code or APIs to extend functionality.

  • Full control over data and infrastructure by hosting on your own servers.

  • Trigger and Event Support: Automate workflows based on various triggers like webhooks, schedules, or external events.

Agentic AI is no longer just a buzzword—it’s actively reshaping how businesses approach task automation and operational efficiency. According to Langchain's Report, 90% of professionals in non-tech companies are already deploying or planning to deploy , almost on par with tech companies at 89%. 

If you're planning to develop Agentic AI to enhance your product, applying Agentic AI framework​s can streamline development and unlock greater scalability.

Need guidance along the way? Lollypop is here to support. As a global UI/UX Design Agency with deep expertise in , we apply design thinking and AI innovation to craft solutions that are not only user-centric but also intelligent and ready for the future.

for a free consultation and let’s explore how AI agents can supercharge your product strategy and elevate your user experience.

items.title: How AI Is Reinventing Developer Onboarding - And Why Every Engineering Leader Should Care
items.author: Panto AI
items.content:
Let’s be honest: onboarding new developers is hard. You want them to hit the ground running, but you also need them to write secure, maintainable code. And in today’s world, “getting up to speed” means more than just learning the codebase. It means understanding business goals, security protocols, and how to collaborate across teams.

If you’re an engineering leader, you know the pain points. According to a recent survey by Stripe, nearly 75% of CTOs say that onboarding is their biggest bottleneck to productivity. Meanwhile, McKinsey reports that companies with strong onboarding processes see 2.5x faster ramp-up for new hires. The message is clear: invest in onboarding, and you’ll see real returns.

But here’s the twist: traditional onboarding just isn’t cutting it anymore.

The New Reality: Onboarding at the Speed of AI

AI is changing the game. Imagine a world where every new hire gets a personalized onboarding experience, tailored to their background, learning style, and project requirements. AI code tools can now analyze past contributions, spot knowledge gaps, and recommend targeted learning resources — all in real time.

Take a cue from Google, where over 25% of code shipped last quarter was AI-generated. That’s not just a fun fact! It’s a signal that AI is now a core part of the developer workflow. And as Satya Nadella, Microsoft’s CEO, has said, “Every business will be a software business, and every company will need to master AI.”

But with great power comes great responsibility. AI-generated code is fast, but it’s not always secure or aligned with your business goals. That’s where the real challenge begins.

Continuous Learning: More Than Just Code

Onboarding isn’t a one-time event. Continuous learning is the secret sauce for keeping teams sharp and secure. Adaptive AI tools can track progress, flag knowledge gaps, and even suggest new training modules, so your team is always learning, always improving.

Security is a top concern for engineering leaders. According to a recent report from Synopsys, 84% of codebases contain at least one open-source vulnerability. AI code reviews and automated security checks are now essential for catching issues before they hit production. As Daniel, a well-known developer, recently demonstrated, it can take less than an hour to exploit vulnerabilities in AI-generated code. That’s a risk no engineering leader can afford.

Who’s Leading the Charge?

The landscape of AI in developer workflows is being shaped by both tech giants and agile innovators. Companies like Microsoft, Google, and AWS are embedding AI deeply into their platforms — think GitHub Copilot, Google’s internal AI coding tools, and AWS’s AI-powered developer assistants. These tools are becoming staples for code completion, automated reviews, and security checks. Meanwhile, open-source pioneers such as Hugging Face and Mistral AI are democratizing access to advanced models, while startups like DeepSeek are pushing the boundaries of efficiency and cost-effectiveness. Industry veterans like Guy Podjarny and Simon Maple are also making waves, championing AI-driven security and DevOps automation. Together, these leaders are setting the pace for how AI will transform developer experiences, making workflows smarter, faster, and more secure than ever before.

The Human-AI Balance: Where Panto Shines

At Panto, we’ve seen firsthand how AI can transform onboarding and continuous learning, but we also know that AI alone isn’t enough. Our platform automates code reviews, enforces security, and ensures business alignment, so your engineers can focus on building, not babysitting code.

What sets us apart? We don’t just automate, we communicate. We help teams understand the “why” behind code changes, so every line of code supports your business goals. The results speak for themselves: teams using Panto have reduced PR merge times by up to 50%.

  • AI-powered onboarding is here to stay. It’s fast, personalized, and effective — but it’s not a silver bullet.

  • Continuous learning is non-negotiable. Adaptive AI tools keep your team sharp and secure.

  • AI code reviews and security checks are essential. They catch vulnerabilities, enforce best practices, and save engineering time.

  • Balance is key. Use AI to automate the boring stuff, but never lose sight of the human connection.

As engineering leaders, we’re at a crossroads. AI is giving us the tools to build faster, smarter teams — but it’s up to us to use them wisely. At Panto, we’re proud to be part of that journey, helping teams ship secure, business-aligned code, one line at a time.

Let’s build the future, together.

Panto can be your new AI Code Review Agent. We are focused on aligning business context with code. Never let bad code reach production again! Try for free today @

items.title: Build an LLM Web App in Python from Scratch: Part 4 (FastAPI, Background Tasks & SSE)
items.author: Zachary Huang
items.content:
Ever asked an AI to write a whole blog post, only to stare at a loading spinner for five minutes, wondering if your browser crashed? We've all been there. Today, we're fixing that. We'll build an AI web app that takes on heavy-duty tasks—like writing a full article—without freezing up. You'll see live progress updates in real-time, so you always know what the AI is up to. Ready to make long-running AI tasks feel fast and interactive? Let's get building!

1. The Spinner of Doom: Why Long AI Tasks Break Your App 😫

Imagine your new AI app is a genius content writer. You ask it to "write an article about space exploration," hit enter, and... the dreaded loading spinner appears. The whole page is frozen. You can't click anything. After a minute, your browser might even give you a "This page is unresponsive" error. Yikes.

This is the classic problem with long-running tasks on the web. Standard web apps work like this:

  1. (e.g., "Generate my article!")
  2. (e.g., Calls the AI, which takes 2-3 minutes)
  3. (The connection is held open, your browser is stuck)
  4. Finally, you get the result. (Or a timeout error!)

This is a terrible user experience. Users need to know their request is being handled and see that progress is being made.

Our Solution: The Smart Restaurant Analogy

Think of it like ordering food at a restaurant:

  • The Bad Way (Standard Web Request): You order a steak. The waiter stands at your table, staring at you without moving, for the entire 15 minutes it takes to cook. Awkward, right? You can't even ask for more water.

  • The Good Way (Our Approach): You order a steak. The waiter says, "Excellent choice! I'll put that in with the chef," and walks away (). A few minutes later, they bring you some bread ("Making progress!"). A bit later, your drink arrives ("Almost ready!"). You're happy and informed while the main course is being prepared in the background.

That's exactly what we're building today. Our app will:

  1. Instantly confirm the user's request.
  2. Offload the heavy AI work to a .
  3. Stream live progress updates back to the user with .

Our toolkit for this mission:

  • : For running the AI job without freezing the app.
  • 🔧 Server-Sent Events (SSE): A simple way to push live updates from the server to the browser.
  • : To organize our multi-step article writing process.

Let's dive into the tools that make this magic possible.

2. Our Tools for the Job: Background Tasks & Server-Sent Events (SSE) 🛠️

To build our responsive AI article writer, we need two key pieces of technology: one to handle the work behind the scenes and another to report on its progress.

FastAPI : The "Work in the Back" Crew

Think of as giving a job to a helper who works independently. You tell FastAPI, "Hey, after you tell the user I got their request, please run this other function in the background."

The magic is that the user gets an immediate response. They don't have to wait for the background work to finish.

It's like ordering from Amazon. You get the "Order Confirmed!" email instantly. The actual process of picking, packing, and shipping your item happens , in the background.

Here's a simple example: sending a welcome email after a user signs up.

  1. When you send a request to , FastAPI sees background_tasks.add_task(...).
  2. It immediately sends back the response. Your browser is happy and responsive.
  3. sending the response, FastAPI runs in the background.

This is perfect for our AI article generator! We'll use it to run the entire AI writing process.

Server-Sent Events (SSE): Your Live Progress Ticker

Okay, so the work is happening in the background. But how do we tell the user what's going on? That's where come in.

SSE is a super simple way for a server to push updates to a browser over a single, one-way connection. It's like a live news ticker: the server sends new headlines as they happen, and your browser just listens.

Why not use WebSockets again? WebSockets (from Part 3) are awesome for two-way chat. But for just sending one-way progress updates, they're a bit like using a walkie-talkie when all you need is a pager. SSE is simpler, lighter, and designed for exactly this "server-to-client" push scenario.

Here's how simple an SSE endpoint is in FastAPI:

And on the browser side, the JavaScript is just as easy:

Waiting for progress...

When you open this page, connects to our endpoint, and the div will update every second. Simple and effective!

3. The AI's To-Do List: A PocketFlow Workflow 📝

So, how does our AI actually write an article? It doesn't happen in one go. We need to give it a step-by-step plan, like a recipe. This is where helps us. It breaks the big job of "write an article" into small, manageable (or steps).

The Central Hub: Our Dictionary

Before we dive into the nodes, let's look at the brain of our operation: a simple Python dictionary. All our nodes will read from and write to this central data hub. It's how they pass information to each other.

Here's what it looks like at the start of a job:

The is our "mailbox." Each node will drop progress updates into it, and our FastAPI server will read from it to update the user.

Step 1: Node

This node's only job is to create the article's structure. (We'll assume a function exists that talks to the AI).

  • grabs the user's from the shared dictionary.
  • takes that and asks the AI for an outline.
  • saves the outline for the next step and—most importantly—drops a progress message into the mailbox. This tells the frontend, "Hey, I'm 33% done!"

This node takes the outline and writes the content for each section.

  • Inside the loop, after each paragraph is written, we immediately send another progress update.
  • This means the user will see the progress bar jump forward multiple times during this step, making the app feel very responsive.

This is the final touch. It takes the combined draft and asks the AI to polish it.

  • This node does the final rewrite.
  • Crucially, it sends the message to the mailbox with . This tells our frontend that the job is finished and the final article is ready!

Tying It All Together with a

Finally, we just need to tell PocketFlow the order of our to-do list.

This is super readable: runs, then , then . This object is what our FastAPI background task will ultimately run.

Here is a visual summary of the entire process:

With our AI's "to-do list" ready, let's connect it to our FastAPI backend.

4. Connecting the Dots: The FastAPI Backend 🔗

Okay, our AI has its "to-do list" from PocketFlow. Now, let's build the web server that acts as the project manager. It will take requests from users, give the work to our AI, and report on the progress.

We'll walk through the main file piece by piece.

First, we need a place to keep track of all the article-writing jobs that are currently running. A simple Python dictionary is perfect for this.

Think of as the front desk of an office. When a new job comes in, we give it a ticket number () and a dedicated mailbox () for all its internal memos.

Part 2: Kicking Off the Job

This is the first thing a user interacts with. They send their article topic to our endpoint, which kicks off the whole process.

  1. job_id = str(uuid.uuid4()) creates a unique ID for this request.
  2. sse_queue = asyncio.Queue() creates the message queue. We then store it in our dictionary using the .
  3. background_tasks.add_task(...) is the magic. It tells FastAPI, "Don't wait! After you send the response, start running the function."
  4. The return {"job_id": job_id} is sent back to the user's browser right away. The user's page doesn't freeze!

Part 3: The Background Worker

This is the function that runs behind the scenes. It's the project manager that actually runs our PocketFlow to-do list.

Here's the crucial connection:

  • It gets the correct (mailbox) for this job from our dictionary.
  • It creates the data dictionary and puts the inside it.
  • When is called, our PocketFlow nodes now have access to this queue and can drop their progress messages into it!

Part 4: Streaming the Progress Updates

While the background task is running, the user's browser connects to our endpoint to listen for updates.

This function looks a bit complex, but it's just a loop that checks the mailbox.

  1. is a special generator that can send () data over time.
  2. It finds the correct mailbox () for the .
  3. The loop starts.
  4. until a message appears in the mailbox.
  5. As soon as a PocketFlow node drops a message in, this line wakes up, grabs the message, and sends it to the browser with .
  6. It keeps doing this until it sees the message, at which point it cleans up and closes the connection.

And that's the whole system! It's a clean loop: the user starts a job, a background worker runs it while dropping messages into a mailbox, and a streamer reads from that mailbox to keep the user updated.

5. Mission Complete! Your App Now Handles the Heavy Lifting 💪

You did it! You've successfully built a web app that can handle long, complex AI tasks without breaking a sweat or frustrating your users. They get an instant response and live progress updates, making the whole experience feel smooth, interactive, and professional.

No more dreaded loading spinners or "page unresponsive" errors. Your app now works like a modern, intelligent assistant: it acknowledges your request, works on it diligently in the background, and keeps you informed every step of the way.

What you conquered today:

  • ✅ Used FastAPI's to offload heavy AI work so your app stays responsive.
  • ✅ Mastered Server-Sent Events (SSE) to stream status updates from the server to the browser in real-time.
  • ✅ Structured a complex, multi-step AI job with PocketFlow, keeping your AI logic separate from your web code.
  • ✅ Used an as a simple "mailbox" to let your background task communicate with your web server.

This architecture is a game-changer for building serious AI applications. You now have the skills to create tools that can generate reports, analyze data, or perform any other time-intensive task, all while keeping your users happy and engaged.

Our "Build an LLM Web App" Journey is Complete!

  • Command-line AI tools ✅
  • Interactive web apps with Streamlit ✅
  • Real-time streaming chat with WebSockets ✅
  • Part 4 (You just crushed it!): Background tasks for heavy AI work ✅

Ready to see it all in action? Grab the complete code, including the HTML and PocketFlow nodes, from our cookbook: . You've leveled up your AI dev skills in a big way. Now go build something amazing! 🚀

items.title: 15 Practical AI Skills I Rely on-You’ll Fall Behind Without Them
items.author: yogesh pant
items.content:
Imagine this: just a year from now, the people making the most money from AI won’t all be developers or marketers. They’ll be regular folks who mastered a few key AI skills — using them to automate, create, and amplify their work.

I know because I’ve done it.

You don’t need a PhD or deep tech skills. You just need curiosity and willingness to experiment.

Here are the 15 AI skills that gave me a serious edge — and the tools I use to practice them every day.
